column5: Designs and manufactures advanced unmanned drones and ground control systems for military applications
column6: Neros Technologies is a U.S.-based defense startup specializing in the design and manufacturing of advanced unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) for military applications. Founded in 2023 and headquartered in El Segundo, California, Neros was established to address the gap in domestic drone production, aiming to restore American leadership in unmanned systems and reduce reliance on foreign supply chains. The company’s product line includes the BlueUAS FPV drone, designed for high-performance flight, long range, and heavy payload capacity, as well as FPV loitering munitions and ground control stations engineered for electronic warfare resistance and extended operational range.Neros vertically integrates its manufacturing process, developing custom flight computers, in-house motor drivers, propulsion components, and proprietary radio systems to ensure secure command, control, and video links with industry-leading jamming resistance. Its drones are rigorously tested in active conflict zones, including Ukraine, to validate battlefield performance and reliability. The company emphasizes rapid iteration, scalability, and cost-effectiveness, with a focus on software-defined hardware that allows for future expansion without dependency on continuous updates.Supported by significant venture funding, Neros is expanding production capacity to meet growing demand from U.S. and allied defense sectors. The company’s mission is to provide practical, scalable unmanned systems that enhance military deterrence and directly contribute to the safety of American and allied personnel.Keywords:unmanned aerial vehicles, military drones, U.S. manufacturing, electronic warfare, battlefield defense, scalable production, secure supply chain, FPV drone, loitering munition, software-defined hardware, El Segundo, vertical integration, jamming resistance, defense technology, rapid iteration

column5: Unified API for accessing and routing LLM models
column6: OpenRouter operates as a unified API platform, functioning as an intermediary service or router for a vast array of Large Language Models (LLMs). Founded in early 2023 by Alex Atallah, co-founder and former CTO of the NFT marketplace OpenSea, and Louis Vichy, the company was established to address the fragmented and rapidly expanding AI model ecosystem. Atallah's journey from leading OpenSea to founding OpenRouter stemmed from his observation of the Cambrian explosion of AI models following the release of ChatGPT and open-source models like Meta's LLaMA. He recognized the emerging need for a streamlined way for developers to navigate and integrate a multitude of specialized models without being locked into a single provider. The company's core business revolves around providing a single, OpenAI-compatible API that allows developers to access and switch between over 400 models from more than 60 providers, including major players like OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and Mistral. This simplifies integration, eliminating the need for developers to write new code or manage multiple contracts for different models. OpenRouter's platform intelligently routes developer prompts to the optimal model based on factors like price, latency, and performance, adding only minimal overhead (~25ms). The business model is usage-based; the company generates revenue by taking a 5% commission on the inference costs that flow through its platform. This approach caters to a wide range of clients, from individual developers and early-stage startups to large multinational corporations routing mission-critical traffic. The platform offers several key features designed to enhance efficiency and reliability for its users, which include over one million developers. It provides unified billing and real-time spending management, allowing for consolidated invoicing across all models. To ensure high availability, the service includes automatic failover and load balancing, redirecting requests if a primary provider is unavailable. OpenRouter also features public leaderboards that offer transparent, real-world data on model usage and performance, which has become a key source of industry trends. For enterprise clients, the platform offers features like zero-logging by default to ensure privacy and the ability to use their own provider keys while still benefiting from OpenRouter's routing and analytics. The company has shown significant traction, reporting an annualized inference spend of over $100 million as of May 2025. This growth attracted substantial investment, with the company securing a combined $40 million in Seed and Series A funding rounds in 2025, led by Andreessen Horowitz and Menlo Ventures, valuing the company at approximately $500 million. column5: Offering Safe and Reliable Transportation for People Needing Assistance
column6: Onward Rides operates as a specialized transportation provider focused on the healthcare sector, addressing transportation as a social determinant of health. The company was co-founded by Mike P. Lewis and Nader Akhnoukh in 2018, with Kim Petty later joining as co-founder and taking on the CEO role. The founders' journey began from personal experiences with aging family members, which highlighted a gap in services for older adults who wished to age in place. Lewis, previously at Airbnb, and Akhnoukh, who co-founded two successful tech companies with Lewis prior to Onward, leveraged their backgrounds in technology and product development to tackle this challenge.  The company's business model centers on two primary offerings: a direct-to-consumer service and a B2B platform for healthcare organizations. For individuals, Onward provides its signature "Companion Rides," a door-through-door service where drivers assist passengers from inside their homes to their destinations. This service is designed for older adults, individuals with disabilities, or those with cognitive and mobility impairments who require more support than typical rideshare services offer. Clients can book rides via a mobile app, website, or by phone. The B2B offering is a transportation management software platform for healthcare systems, PACE (Programs of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly) programs, and transit agencies. This platform allows care coordinators to order, manage, and track various levels of transportation for their patients from a single interface, integrating with partners like the VA Health System, UCSF Health, and Lucile Packard Children's Hospital Stanford. Revenue is generated through ride fees, which can be paid by individuals or covered by partner healthcare organizations, and likely through software subscription fees for its management platform.  Onward's core service distinguishes itself by training its "Companion Drivers" to handle mobility aids, provide physical assistance, and offer companionship. Drivers are trained in first aid, CPR, and dementia support, addressing a key need for safety and trust. The service platform includes features like an algorithm for matching riders with suitable drivers, the ability to request a favorite driver for recurring appointments, and real-time ride notifications for family members or caregivers. This high-touch approach has been shown to reduce clinic no-shows and decrease the time healthcare staff spend coordinating transportation. Initially launched in the San Francisco Bay Area, the company has since expanded its operations to Sacramento, Los Angeles, and Phoenix. column5: Metals refinement company extracting value from waste in the mining industry called Bauxite Residue ('Red Mud"​)
column6: Phoenix Tailings is a pioneering startup that operates in the metals production industry with a unique, sustainable approach. The company's mission is to revolutionize the way metals are produced by extracting valuable metals and rare earth elements from mining waste, thereby reducing environmental impact and creating a cleaner supply chain. The company's target market includes industries that rely heavily on metals, such as the renewable energy sector, which is increasingly dependent on metals and rare earth elements. As the world shifts towards an electrified industrial ecosystem, the demand for these materials is on the rise. However, traditional methods of metals production are environmentally destructive and often linked to poor labor conditions. Phoenix Tailings aims to address these issues by offering a more sustainable alternative.Phoenix Tailings' business model is based on leveraging zero-waste technology to transform metals production. Their process uses clean energy sources and avoids hazardous chemicals, resulting in no waste. This not only reduces the environmental costs associated with traditional metals production but also unlocks the full value of natural resources. The company generates revenue by selling the metals and rare earth elements it extracts from mining waste. This approach not only provides a more sustainable source of these materials but also helps to reduce the environmental impact of mining operations.In summary, Phoenix Tailings is a forward-thinking startup that is changing the metals production industry by offering a sustainable, zero-waste solution. Keywords: Sustainable, Metals Production, Zero-Waste Technology, Clean Energy, Rare Earth Elements, Mining Waste, Environmentally Friendly, Supply Chain, Renewable Energy, Natural Resources.

column5: Making the software supply chain secure by default
column6: Chainguard is a startup that operates in the supply chain risk management sector. It provides a unique service of conducting audits to identify both internal and external supply chain risks. The company then provides detailed reports with suggested remediations, improvements, and next steps to help businesses mitigate these risks. Chainguard's clients are businesses that rely on supply chains, particularly those that use software supply chains.The company's business model is centered around providing a service that enhances the integrity of software supply chains. They do this by offering a curated base container image distro, which is essentially a version of a software package that has been carefully selected and organized. This allows businesses to quickly and easily change their software over to use the Chainguard Image, which is designed to be secure and efficient.Chainguard makes money by charging for these audits and the subsequent reports. They also generate revenue from the use of their curated base container image distro. The company recently raised $61 million, indicating strong investor confidence in its business model and growth potential.In addition to audits, Chainguard also offers supply chain observability services. This allows businesses to know what's running, where it came from, and what dependencies they have. This service is particularly useful for businesses that need to meet industry standards and frameworks.Keywords: Supply Chain, Risk Management, Audits, Software Supply Chain, Base Container Image Distro, Chainguard Image, Supply Chain Observability, Industry Standards, Remediations, Business Model.

column5: The business management platform powering the next generation of premium salons and spas
column6: Boulevard is a technology startup that offers a comprehensive business management platform for service-based businesses, particularly in the beauty and wellness industry. The platform provides a suite of tools that streamline operations, from booking appointments to managing inventory. It is designed to enhance the customer experience, with features such as customizable intake forms, two-way messaging, self-check-in, and automated arrival alerts. The platform also offers detailed client profiles, which include information on past visits, previous purchases, and social profiles. This allows businesses to personalize their services and deepen their relationships with their clients. The platform's one-tap tipping feature is designed to encourage upsells, pre-bookings, and increase staff gratuity.Boulevard operates on a Software as a Service (SaaS) business model, which means it makes money by charging its clients a subscription fee to use its platform. The company's clients are primarily businesses in the beauty and wellness industry, including hair salons, spas, and beauty labs. The company has received positive feedback from industry leaders, who praise the platform's ease of use, its ability to enhance the customer experience, and its effectiveness in improving operational efficiency. column5: Innovative talent curation firm that is changing the face of recruiting by blending technology with recruiter expertise
column6: Riviera Partners is a specialized recruiting firm focused on placing top-tier talent in engineering, product, and design roles. Operating primarily in the technology sector, the company serves a diverse range of clients, from emerging startups to established tech giants. Riviera Partners leverages its deep industry knowledge and extensive network to match highly skilled candidates with companies that require expertise in building large-scale systems, often sourced from industry leaders like Google and Apple. With offices in major tech hubs such as San Francisco, Silicon Valley, Los Angeles, New York, and Denver, Riviera Partners has a broad reach and local expertise, enabling it to engage high-caliber candidates effectively. The company is committed to diversity and strives to create impactful matches that benefit both the candidate and the client. column5: Advanced autonomous manufacturing for critical defense components, enhancing U.S. defense capabilities and supply chain resilience
column6: Deterrence is a cutting-edge company specializing in advanced autonomous manufacturing of critical energetic components, primarily serving the U.S. defense industry. Operating within the defense sector, Deterrence addresses the pressing manufacturing constraints faced by the industry, which are increasingly becoming problematic. The company utilizes adaptive software to optimize the entire energetic manufacturing process, from chemistry to equipment fabrication, ensuring top-quality production from raw materials to finished products. By deploying advanced robotics and intelligent automation, Deterrence replicates human dexterity with robotic precision, enhancing production efficiency and safety.Deterrence's business model focuses on revitalizing domestic manufacturing capacity to strengthen U.S. and allied defense capabilities. The company generates revenue by providing state-of-the-art manufacturing solutions that contribute to a more responsive defense supply chain ecosystem. By building faster and safer energetics production, Deterrence plays a crucial role in preventing global conflict and ensuring national security.Keywords: autonomous manufacturing, defense components, U.S. defense, robotics, intelligent automation, supply chain, manufacturing constraints, national security, energetic components, domestic manufacturing.

column5: Building new tools and methods to image microbes and describe their environments for research
column6: Kanvas Bio operates at the intersection of advanced imaging technology and microbiome research to revolutionize therapeutic discovery and human health. The company’s core offering is a full-stack technology platform that provides detailed spatial mapping of host-microbiome interactions. This platform enables researchers to visualize and understand the complex relationships between the human body and its microbiome, which has been a largely unexplored area until now. Kanvas Bio serves pharmaceutical companies, research institutions, and healthcare providers who are focused on developing new treatments and interventions based on microbiome science. The company operates in the biotechnology and healthcare markets, where there is a growing interest in microbiome-based therapies. The business model of Kanvas Bio involves providing its advanced imaging tools and data analytics as a service to its clients. Additionally, the company is developing its own pipeline of live biotherapeutics designed to modulate the microbiome for various health conditions. Revenue is generated through service contracts, licensing agreements, and potentially through the commercialization of its proprietary therapeutics. Kanvas Bio is headquartered in Princeton, NJ, and San Francisco, CA, and is committed to inclusivity, diversity, and respect within its team. Keywords: microbiome, spatial mapping, imaging technology, therapeutic discovery, biotherapeutics, healthcare, biotechnology, data analytics, pharmaceutical, research institutions.

column5: Full-building operating system of software, products, and services designed to make every building better
column6: Latch is a company that provides smart access systems for apartment buildings, commercial offices, and other properties. Their products include smart locks, intercoms, and cameras that can be managed through a single software platform. The system is designed to eliminate the need for physical keys and provide a more seamless and secure experience for residents, property managers, and other stakeholders.For residents, Latch offers the convenience of unlocking their doors with a smartphone, passcode, or keycard. They can also grant temporary access to guests, dog walkers, and other service providers remotely. For property managers, Latch provides a centralized platform for managing access to all doors in a building, as well as tools for monitoring and auditing access events. This can help to improve security, reduce operational costs, and enhance the resident experience.Latch's business model is based on a combination of hardware sales and recurring software revenue. The company sells its smart locks and other hardware to property owners and developers, and then charges a monthly or annual subscription fee for access to its software platform. This recurring revenue stream provides a predictable and scalable source of income for the company.Keywords: smart access, property management, security, keyless entry, real estate technology, access control, SaaS, IoT, hardware, commercial real estate

column5: Security-first BCDR platform for managed service providers
column6: Slide is a Business Continuity & Disaster Recovery (BCDR) company that develops solutions exclusively for Managed Service Providers (MSPs). The company was founded by Austin McChord, the founder and former CEO of Datto, and Michael Fass, Datto's former General Counsel & Chief People Officer. They stated they felt they had "unfinished business" after Datto's sale and wanted to bring innovation back to the MSP channel. The firm officially launched in February 2025. McChord started his first company, Datto, in 2007 from his father's basement while still in college, growing it into a leading MSP solutions provider that was acquired for approximately $1.5 billion in 2017. Fass was an executive leader at Datto for nine years, seeing it grow from a 100-employee startup to a publicly-traded enterprise. Both founders are also involved with Outsiders Fund, an early-stage venture capital firm that has backed Slide. Slide's business model is 100% channel-focused, meaning it sells exclusively through MSPs. The company provides a modern BCDR platform built from scratch, free from the constraints of legacy code. Its product portfolio is designed around security, performance, and simplicity, featuring a cloud-first architecture, all-flash storage, and native data encryption at rest and in transit at no extra cost. The platform's offerings include block-level backups, backup verification, instant virtualization, and an "Active Reclaim" feature that optimizes storage by reclaiming unused space. Slide offers physical appliances, including the Z1, R1, and B1 models, with capacities ranging from 1TB to 150TB. The company is headquartered in Norwalk, Connecticut. In June 2025, Slide announced it had raised $25 million in a Series A funding round led by Base10 Partners, with participation from Outsiders Fund and Top Down Ventures. This funding is intended to scale platform development and support its expansion into Canada, where it has established a new data center to meet data residency requirements. Keywords: Business Continuity, Disaster Recovery, BCDR, Managed Service Providers, MSP, data protection, data backup, cloud backup, security-first architecture, channel-focused, Austin McChord, Michael Fass, Datto, backup appliance, hybrid cloud backup, data encryption, IT channel

column5: Focuses on providing secure infrastructure to power real economies in digital experiences and environments
column6: ZEBEDEE, legally ZEBEDEE LLC, operates as a financial technology company with a sharp focus on the gaming industry, providing a platform for processing payments. The company was established in 2019 by Simon Cowell, André Neves, and Christian Moss. The founding team combines diverse expertise: Simon Cowell, the CEO, has a background in corporate development and investment management; André Neves, the CTO, is a software engineer with a passion for the Bitcoin Lightning Network; and Christian Moss, Head of R&D, is recognized as a pioneer in Bitcoin gaming.The core of ZEBEDEE's business is a payment processing platform that empowers game developers to integrate real-money economies into their games. This is achieved by allowing any in-game event to trigger an instant micropayment. The company utilizes the Bitcoin Lightning Network to facilitate these transactions, which can be as small as a fraction of a cent, with minimal fees. ZEBEDEE's revenue model is based on a 1% fee on all in-game transactions. The company serves game developers and studios of all sizes, from independent creators to large corporations like Square Enix, which is also an investor.ZEBEDEE offers a suite of products for both developers and gamers. For developers, the primary offering is a developer dashboard with API-based tools and an SDK for integrating Bitcoin rewards into games. For gamers, the company provides the ZBD app, which functions as a custodial wallet for sending, receiving, and earning Bitcoin through gameplay and other activities. The platform supports a variety of games, including titles like Counter-Strike: Global Offensive, as well as casual mobile games. Beyond gaming, ZEBEDEE has expanded its ecosystem to include Bitcoin-rewarded shopping through partnerships with companies like Rewards Bunny. The company has also developed tools like ZBD Bots for Discord and Telegram, and ZBD Streamer for content creators to receive tips.Keywords: gaming payments, fintech, Bitcoin Lightning Network, micropayments, in-game economy, payment processor, developer tools, API, SDK, crypto gaming, real-money rewards, virtual worlds, global payments, Andre Neves, Simon Cowell, Christian Moss, ZBD app, rewarded gaming, payment infrastructure, digital transactions

column5: Electric energy distribution through their energy demand management software services
column6: DemandQ, formerly known as eCurv Inc., operates as a key player in the smart energy management sector, leveraging artificial intelligence to optimize electricity consumption for commercial clients. Founded in 2011 by Jon Stah, the company is headquartered in Massachusetts, US. Gary Morsches currently leads the company as CEO. The firm's leadership also includes Jerry Ventura, President and CTO, who brings over two decades of enterprise software experience from companies like Dynatrace and BEZ Systems. The core of DemandQ's business is its patented, cloud-based software platform that aims to reduce electricity costs, particularly the peak demand charges that can constitute 30-50% of a commercial electricity bill. The business model is primarily Software-as-a-Service (SaaS), offering solutions without requiring upfront capital investment from clients. DemandQ serves a diverse commercial and industrial client base, including retail stores, office buildings, warehouses, quick-serve restaurants, and telecommunications facilities. It also partners with energy suppliers and utilities to offer incentive programs for intelligent energy management. DemandQ's platform integrates with existing building automation systems, HVAC units, and electric vehicle charging stations. Using its patented Queued Power Access Control (QPAC) technology and predictive analytics, the system intelligently orchestrates when and how high-consumption equipment operates to avoid simultaneous power draws that lead to costly demand spikes. This process occurs automatically without compromising occupant comfort or facility operations. Key services include Intelligent Demand Optimization, Price Mitigation which responds to real-time energy market prices, and Automated Demand Response (ADR) to ensure compliance during grid peak demand events. The company claims its technology can reduce electricity costs by around 10% and has delivered over $100 million in economic benefits. Keywords: energy management, demand response, cleantech, SaaS, electricity cost reduction, peak demand management, building automation, AI energy optimization, smart grid, intelligent demand, energy efficiency, utility cost savings, commercial energy solutions, grid stability, energy analytics, carbon footprint reduction, load management, EV charging optimization, HVAC optimization, facility energy management

column5: Global satellite 5G IoT and direct-to-device connectivity
column6: OQ Technology is a global satellite operator pioneering 5G Internet of Things (IoT) and direct-to-device (D2D) connectivity through a constellation of low Earth orbit (LEO) satellites. Founded in 2016 by Omar Qaise, the company is headquartered in Luxembourg and aims to bridge the digital divide by providing seamless 5G coverage in remote and rural areas where terrestrial networks are unavailable. The company functions as a global satellite 5G IoT operator, providing uninterrupted cellular coverage for machines and assets anywhere on the planet. The founder and CEO, Omar Qaise, has an extensive background in the satellite industry, having worked for the German Aerospace Center (DLR), the European Space Agency (ESA), and SES. This experience in both the technical and business aspects of satellite services inspired him to create OQ Technology, recognizing a market need for affordable narrowband IoT communication via smaller, more cost-effective satellites. He established the company as a solo founder with the vision of integrating 5G standards with satellite communication to connect industrial assets beyond the reach of traditional cell towers. OQ Technology's core service provides 5G non-terrestrial network (NTN) connectivity that is compliant with 3GPP standards, allowing standard 5G smartphones and NB-IoT devices to connect directly to its satellites without requiring custom hardware. This approach enables interoperability with billions of existing cellular devices and significantly reduces deployment costs for customers. The company's satellite constellation functions as 'cell towers in the sky', extending reliable connectivity for a variety of applications. Key services include wellhead monitoring, asset tracking, drone controls, vehicle telematics, and emergency broadcast messaging. In July 2025, the company successfully transmitted the first-ever image over its 5G NTN IoT satellite network, a milestone that expands its capabilities to include visual surveillance for industries like energy, defense, and agriculture. The company's business model involves partnering with global telecom operators to extend their coverage and selling services directly to enterprise customers in sectors such as energy, mining, logistics, maritime, and agriculture. OQ Technology has secured commercial contracts with major entities, including a significant agreement with one of the world's largest oil and gas companies to monitor their infrastructure. The company has steadily grown its satellite constellation, with 10 satellites in orbit and plans for further expansion. OQ Technology has successfully raised capital through multiple funding rounds, including a €13 million Series A in 2022 and is in the process of a Series B round backed by the European Innovation Council and the Luxembourg government. Keywords: satellite 5G, IoT connectivity, direct-to-device, D2D, non-terrestrial network, NTN, LEO constellation, narrowband IoT, NB-IoT, 3GPP standards, remote area connectivity, machine-to-machine, M2M, asset tracking, oil and gas, agriculture technology, maritime communication, logistics, emergency services, satellite communication, SpaceTech, global connectivity, satellite operator, cubesats, 5G NTN

column5: Helps e-store owners ship, track and manage returns from a single screen
column6: Torod is a dynamic startup that specializes in automating shipping operations for businesses, particularly those in the e-commerce sector. The company provides a platform that connects clients with multiple shipping delivery services, offering a variety of same-day delivery options. This allows businesses to streamline their shipping processes, making them more efficient and cost-effective.Torod serves a wide range of clients, from small online merchants to large-scale e-commerce businesses. The platform is designed to cater to different stages of business growth, offering flexible pricing plans to suit varying needs. These plans range from a free option for those needing a quick and easy solution, to more advanced plans for high-volume shippers requiring batch and automation workflows.The market Torod operates in is the logistics and shipping industry, which is crucial for e-commerce businesses. By providing a centralized platform to track all shipping expenses and manage multiple delivery services, Torod addresses a significant pain point for online merchants: the complexity and cost of shipping.Torod's business model is subscription-based, with different pricing tiers that cater to the specific needs of its clients. This model ensures a steady stream of revenue while providing value to businesses by reducing their shipping costs and improving delivery efficiency. Additionally, Torod partners with various companies, such as Urpay wallet merchants, to embed delivery solutions, further expanding its market reach.In summary, Torod simplifies the shipping process for e-commerce businesses by offering a comprehensive platform that connects multiple delivery services, tracks expenses, and provides flexible pricing plans. column5: Automated solution for continuous scanning of environments to detect and protect against exposed secrets like private keys and credentials
column6: TruffleSecurity is a cybersecurity startup that offers a unique solution to protect sensitive information within a company's digital infrastructure. Their primary product, TruffleHog, is a scanning tool that searches for sensitive keys in hidden content and version history from commonly used tools across a company. This means it helps businesses find and secure potentially vulnerable data that could be exploited by malicious actors.TruffleHog's open-source engine verifies over 700 unique credential types against the key provider, which helps to eliminate false positives. This means it can accurately identify real threats and reduce the time and resources wasted on false alarms. Furthermore, TruffleHog automates the secret remediation process, which means it helps to quickly fix any security issues that it finds.TruffleSecurity operates in the cybersecurity market, serving clients who need to protect their sensitive data. This includes businesses of all sizes, across various industries. Their business model is likely based on a subscription or license fee for using TruffleHog, although specific details are not provided.The company prides itself on transparency and openness, with their scanning engine and integrations being open source. column6: ButterflyMX operates in the property management technology sector, providing a comprehensive access control system for real estate properties. column5: AI-powered voice-to-sticker printer for children's creativity
column6: Hapiko is a Brooklyn-based creative technology company focused on developing products for children. The company's flagship product, Stickerbox, is an AI-powered, voice-to-sticker printer designed to foster creativity and hands-on play for kids without the need for a smartphone or laptop. Children can speak their ideas, such as "a dinosaur on a skateboard," and the device instantly prints a black-and-white sticker of the creation for them to color and customize. The company was founded by CEO Arun Gupta and CTO Robert Whitney. In November 2025, Hapiko announced it had raised a $7 million funding round led by Maveron and Serena Ventures, founded by tennis champion Serena Williams. This funding coincided with the official launch of Stickerbox. The business model centers on the direct sale of the Stickerbox device, which is priced at $99.99 in the United States. The company plans to use its funding to scale production and expand its market reach. Stickerbox is engineered with a strong emphasis on safety and privacy. It operates with minimal data collection, features no camera, and includes built-in content moderation to ensure all generated images are age-appropriate. The printer uses thermal, ink-free technology with BPA- and BPS-free paper. Hapiko's mission is to merge AI with real-world play, providing tools that enhance childhood creativity rather than promoting passive screen time. column5: Perimenopause health platform redefining women's midlife care
column6: Play Health is a digital health platform focused on redefining midlife care for women by addressing the challenges of perimenopause. The company was founded in 2025 by Andrea Mazzocchi, PhD, and Ian Miller, who are second-time Y Combinator-backed founders. Mazzocchi, the CEO, previously founded and served as CEO of Known Medicine (YC S20), a cancer precision medicine company that was acquired by Pathos AI in 2023. Her background is in tissue engineering and tumor microenvironments. Miller, the CTO, co-founded the unicorn company Gecko Robotics (YC W16) and later led automation and engineering at Known Medicine and Pathos AI. Play Health targets the perimenopause market, which affects over 30 million women in the U.S. and is often overlooked in women's health. The company's platform combines personalized symptom and cycle tracking, at-home hormone testing, and AI-driven content. This integrated approach provides women with actionable, personalized insights to help manage symptoms and improve their quality of life. The business model aims to deliver personalized care directly to women while simultaneously building a foundational longitudinal dataset for perimenopause. This data is intended to drive the discovery of new diagnostics, therapeutics, and future standards of care for women's midlife health. Keywords: perimenopause, menopause, women's health, digital health, femtech, at-home testing, hormone health, AI-driven health, personalized care, symptom tracking, midlife health, Y Combinator, health technology, preventative care, diagnostics, therapeutics

column5: Technology to empower sports venues to streamline reservations, manage events, and run spaces autonomously
column6: PodPlay Technologies provides a specialized vertical SaaS platform for recreational sports venues, originating as the technology powering PingPod's autonomous table tennis clubs. The company was formally established as a spin-off from its parent, PingPod Inc., in 2023 to commercialize its proprietary tech stack. This strategic move followed a period of significant growth and market validation within PingPod, which was co-founded in 2019 by Max Kogler (CEO), David Silberman (CFO), and Ernesto Ebuen (CPO). Kogler brought experience from the hedge fund world and previous startups, Silberman hailed from equity research at UBS, and Ebuen is a former U.S. top-ranked table tennis player. PodPlay itself is led by a team that includes Kogler, CTO Ilya Rivkin, formerly of Goldman Sachs' Marcus, and CRO Ben Borton, an early investor in PingPod. The business model centers on providing a white-label, integrated software and hardware solution that enables venue operators to automate and streamline their facilities. PodPlay generates revenue through a tiered subscription service for its SaaS platform. Clients range from operators of pickleball, padel, and ping pong courts to pool halls and golf simulators. The company's key value proposition is the ability to run venues autonomously, reducing labor costs by as much as 66% and allowing for 24/7 operation. This autonomous model proved resilient during the COVID-19 pandemic, as its contactless nature allowed for early reopening. The PodPlay platform is a comprehensive operating system that integrates reservations, event and membership management, coaching directories, analytics, and payments processing. This core software is enhanced by optional, proprietary hardware-enabled features. These include digital scoreboards, instant video replays that customers can save and share, and secure, remote door access, which is a cornerstone of the autonomous model. The platform is offered in several tiers—from a basic white-label app to a full "Autonomous" plan—allowing clients to select the level of automation and features that best suits their business. This has attracted notable clients in the rapidly expanding pickleball market, such as Pickleball Kingdom and CityPickle. To accelerate its growth, PodPlay secured $8 million in a Series A funding round in late 2025, led by Frontier Growth. Keywords: recreational venue management, autonomous sports clubs, vertical SaaS, pickleball technology, sports facility software, racket sports technology, club automation, member management software, court booking system, digital scoreboards, instant video replay, sports tech, venue operations, Max Kogler, David Silberman, Ernesto Ebuen, PingPod, facility access control, sports analytics platform, event management software, coaching platform

column5: Leading Indian agriculture towards a more structured and technology driven ecosystem
column6: AgroStar is a pioneering AgTech startup dedicated to transforming the agricultural landscape in India. The company operates with a mission to "Help Farmers Win" by providing comprehensive agricultural solutions throughout the crop life cycle. AgroStar serves Indian farmers, offering them a range of services designed to increase crop yield, reduce input costs, and improve the quality of their produce. The company operates in the Indian agricultural market, which is vast and diverse, catering to millions of farmers across various states. AgroStar's business model is built on an omnichannel approach, meaning they provide services through multiple channels including an Agri Advisory Center, the AgroStar Agri Doctor app, and physical retail stores. This model ensures that farmers can access AgroStar's services in the most convenient way for them.AgroStar makes money by selling high-quality agricultural inputs such as seeds, fertilizers, and pesticides. These products can be ordered through their app or purchased at their retail stores. Additionally, the company offers personalized advisory services powered by extensive data and agronomy knowledge, helping farmers make informed decisions to boost their productivity. Post-harvest, AgroStar also assists farmers in accessing both domestic and global markets to secure the best prices for their produce.The company's platform leverages state-of-the-art technology developed in-house by their product and engineering teams. This tech-enabled platform disseminates valuable agronomic information and provides scientific and sustainable farming advice. By bridging the knowledge gap and offering personalized assistance, AgroStar significantly impacts farmers' lives, enabling them to grow more and earn more.In summary, AgroStar is revolutionizing Indian agriculture by providing end-to-end solutions that empower farmers to achieve better yields and higher profits.Keywords: AgTech, Indian farmers, crop yield, input costs, omnichannel, advisory services, agricultural inputs, technology, market access, sustainable farming.

column5: Offering curated selections and exclusive designs of electronic products
column6: Drop, formerly known as Massdrop, is an American e-commerce company based in San Francisco, California, that was acquired by Corsair in July 2023. The company operates as a community-driven commerce platform where enthusiasts of various product categories, particularly mechanical keyboards and audiophile headphones, can connect, discuss, and purchase products.Drop's business model is centered around using data and insights from its community to develop and bring to market new products. This is often done through collaborations with manufacturers to create "Drop exclusive" items or by running "group buys" where a product is made available for purchase once a certain number of people commit to buying it, often at a discounted price. This approach allows the company to gauge demand before production and offer niche products that might not be viable in a traditional retail model.The company serves a global market of tech-savvy consumers and hobbyists who are passionate about high-quality, often customizable, gear. Its revenue is generated through the direct sale of these products on its website. column5: Analog pioneers AI-driven edge computing, seamlessly linking people, places, and things with smart sensors and mixed reality devices
column6: Analog, founded and led by CEO Alex Kipman, was officially launched in January 2024 at the AI House in Davos. Kipman, a prominent technologist known for his extensive work at Microsoft on projects like the HoloLens and Xbox Kinect, established Analog to pioneer solutions where technology amplifies human capabilities rather than replacing them. The company, headquartered in Abu Dhabi, is a venture backed by G42, a UAE-based technology group. Analog operates as a solutions provider focused on the intersection of artificial intelligence and edge computing, aiming to create seamless connections between people, places, and things. The firm's business model is centered on collaborating with partners across various sectors to deliver custom solutions for complex global challenges. Its target clients include industries like entertainment, sports, and public sector entities, particularly those involved in smart city development. Analog's approach moves intelligence from the cloud to the edge, processing data from live, real-time sensors to enable faster and more responsive systems. This strategy is designed to reduce latency and improve operational efficiency for its clients. The company's services revolve around integrating smart sensors and mixed reality to build immersive, interactive environments. One of its key projects involves a partnership with VERSES AI to develop smart city infrastructure in the UAE, focusing on optimizing urban mobility and transportation through sensor data and simulations. In the entertainment sector, Analog collaborates with Nova Sky Stories and Abu Dhabi's Department of Culture and Tourism to produce large-scale drone shows, creating holographic-like stories in the night sky. For sports, the company has partnered with UAE Team ADQ and UAE Team Emirates XRG to use adaptive intelligence and sensor data to enhance athlete performance. At its core, Analog's technology is designed with a human-centric philosophy, ensuring that its powerful AI and computing solutions are intuitive and serve to enrich human experiences. Keywords: Alex Kipman, edge computing, human-centric AI, mixed reality, smart sensors, G42, Abu Dhabi, smart city infrastructure, real-time data processing, immersive experiences, VERSES AI partnership, Nova Sky Stories, UAE Team ADQ, UAE Team Emirates XRG, adaptive intelligence, IoT solutions, data-driven solutions, sensor data analytics, HoloLens creator

column6: Luminal provides a serverless cloud platform for AI companies, focusing on deploying artificial intelligence models with enhanced speed and reliability. The company, which participated in Y Combinator's Summer 2025 batch, has developed an open-source machine learning compiler that generates optimized GPU code directly from PyTorch, aiming to accelerate the transition of models from research to production. The platform's key features include auto-scaling, auto-batching, and on-demand weight streaming to manage variable traffic and reduce engineering overhead. Founded in 2025 by Joe Fioti, Matthew Gunton, and Jake Stevens, Luminal is headquartered in San Francisco. The founding team combines expertise in hardware and software engineering. Fioti, the CEO, previously worked at Intel on CPU microcode and ML accelerators. Gunton's background includes engineering roles at Amazon, where he developed systems for the company's fulfillment network. Stevens has experience from Apple and as a founder with a previous exit. Luminal aims to solve the complexity and cost associated with deploying and optimizing AI models, a process that traditionally requires significant engineering effort. It has secured $500K in funding from its participation in the Y Combinator accelerator program. The company's core product is an ML framework and compiler that automatically discovers and generates complex GPU kernel code, such as Flash Attention, without manual engineering. This allows for the deployment of any model with claims of significant speed-up and reduced idle GPU costs. It is designed to be a drop-in upgrade to PyTorch, making it accessible to AI researchers and ML engineers. Luminal's technology is built in Rust and interacts directly with CUDA and Metal APIs, avoiding layers of abstraction to maintain high performance. Keywords: serverless cloud, AI model deployment, GPU code generation, machine learning compiler, PyTorch optimization, Y Combinator, deep learning framework, AI infrastructure, CUDA kernels, model performance

column5: Developing state-of-the-art foundation models for structured reasoning using deep learning, by building models that manipulate structured data and learn algebraic structure with interpretable and verifiable logic
column6: Symbolica is pioneering a new frontier in artificial intelligence by leveraging the principles of category theory to reshape the foundations of machine learning. This innovative approach addresses the inherent limitations observed in existing large language models like ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini, which include costly training processes, complex deployment, challenging validation, and susceptibility to producing inaccurate or irrelevant information ("hallucinations").At the core of Symbolica’s technology is the use of category theory, a branch of mathematics that deals with abstract structure and relationships, to develop AI models capable of structured and inductive reasoning. This mathematical framework allows Symbolica’s models to perform complex tasks such as code synthesis and theorem proving with higher accuracy and efficiency. The models are designed to understand and manipulate algebraic structures, thus moving beyond mere token prediction to true structured reasoning.Symbolica is setting a new standard in the AI field with its emphasis on compliance and interpretability. The company aims to eradicate unstructured model outputs and reduce hallucinations, making AI outputs explainable and verifiable. This level of control and transparency is crucial for applications where trust and reliability are paramount, such as in legal, medical, and financial services.Furthermore, Symbolica enhances the efficiency of AI training and deployment. By integrating structure directly into the inputs, outputs, and reasoning processes of their models, these models require smaller datasets and less computational power, leading to faster training times and more rapid deployment capabilities. This efficiency does not only reduce costs but also accelerates time to market for AI solutions, offering significant competitive advantages.

column5: Through technology-enabled services, we use data to achieve smarter marketing for the world’s largest brands.
column6: Founded in 2006 by David Kidder, Clickable emerged in New York City with the mission to simplify online advertising for marketers and agencies. The company developed a software-as-a-service (SaaS) platform designed to provide powerful intelligence for social and search advertising campaigns. This web service enabled users to manage search marketing, automate reporting, and handle ad buying across multiple networks like Google, Facebook, and Bing through a single, intuitive dashboard.Clickable's platform was engineered to consolidate complex data from various advertising channels, translating it into meaningful metrics and customer insights. This allowed clients to optimize their digital marketing spend, prove return on investment, and make more profitable advertising decisions. The service catered to a range of clients, from small and midsize businesses to large agencies within networks like WPP and Interpublic Group, and brands such as Ann Taylor and Etsy. Revenue was generated through its suite of online advertising management tools.The company's journey included significant financial backing from prominent investors. Over seven funding rounds, Clickable raised a total of $32.6 million. Key investors included Founders Fund, Union Square Ventures, FirstMark Capital, and American Express. A notable Series B round in 2008, led by Founders Fund, secured $14.5 million, which was earmarked to accelerate product innovation and commercial expansion. By 2012, the firm had grown to a team of around 70 employees.In June 2012, Clickable was acquired by Syncapse, a social media management company, in a deal valued at approximately $33 million, primarily in equity. The acquisition aimed to integrate Clickable's paid media and ad-buying technology with Syncapse's expertise in earned media analytics, creating a more comprehensive social marketing platform. Following the acquisition, Clickable's co-founder and CEO, David Kidder, transitioned to a strategic advisor role for Syncapse. Subsequently, in 2019, Brand Networks acquired the assets of Clickable. Today, the Clickable brand operates as a subsidiary of LookSmart Ltd., continuing to offer customized marketing intelligence solutions.Keywords: search advertising, social advertising, PPC management, advertising intelligence, marketing analytics, ad tech, campaign management, David Kidder, Syncapse, Brand Networks, marketing ROI, digital advertising platform, customer insights, data automation, online ad management, paid media, ad buying, search marketing, web service, business intelligence, American Express Ventures, Founders Fund, Union Square Ventures

column6: Zvilo is a digital lending platform that operates in the realm of sustainable trade, primarily in emerging markets. Its primary objective is to unlock capital that is otherwise inaccessible due to various market constraints. The company achieves this by providing liquidity and savings across different value chains, starting from suppliers to the end buyer. In simpler terms, Zvilo helps businesses access money that is tied up in unpaid invoices, allowing them to continue operating and growing without the need for additional collateral.Zvilo's business model is designed to serve suppliers who often struggle to access affordable credit. The company's product allows these suppliers to convert their unpaid invoices into accessible capital, which can be used to meet their working capital needs and fuel business growth. This approach not only helps individual businesses but also stimulates economic activity in the broader market.Zvilo operates in a niche market, focusing on emerging economies where traditional financial services may not be as readily available or accessible. By leveraging digital technology, Zvilo is able to reach these underserved markets and provide a much-needed financial service.The company makes money by charging a fee for its services. When a supplier uses Zvilo to access capital tied up in unpaid invoices, Zvilo charges a percentage of the invoice amount as a service fee. This fee-based model allows Zvilo to generate revenue while providing a valuable service to its clients.Keywords: Digital Lending, Sustainable Trade, Emerging Markets, Liquidity, Savings, Value Chains, Suppliers, Unpaid Invoices, Accessible Capital, Fee-Based Revenue.

column5: PLA provides software subscription services to livestock producers, brokers, risk managers, nutritionists and animal nutrition companies
column6: Performance Livestock Analytics (PLA) offers a cloud-based data and analytics platform tailored for the beef production industry, aiming to enhance profitability and sustainability for independent livestock producers. The company was founded in 2015 by Dane Kuper and Dustin Balsley, both of whom have roots in Iowa farming communities. Their journey combines hands-on agricultural experience with insights from working at technology companies in Silicon Valley, which inspired them to address the technology gap in the livestock sector compared to row crop farming. The firm's business model is centered on a software-as-a-service (SaaS) subscription, providing cattle producers with tools to digitize and automate on-farm data collection. PLA’s flagship product, Performance Beef, is an application that allows users to monitor animal nutrition, health, and operational and financial data to improve feed management. It helps producers change rations, create invoices, track individual animal health, and analyze performance trends. A complementary product, Cattle Krush, utilizes real-time market data to provide producers with instant breakeven analyses and profit alerts, assisting in the strategic timing of buying and selling cattle. The platform serves independent cattle feedlots and beef producers in the United States, Canada, and other international markets. PLA's history includes a significant milestone in April 2020 when it was acquired by Zoetis, a global animal health company, to bolster Zoetis's precision livestock farming offerings. However, by July 2023, PLA separated from Zoetis to operate as an independent entity once again. This move was supported by a new funding round led by Builders VC, one of PLA's original investors, enabling the company to accelerate growth, expand into new markets, and further develop its product suite with increased autonomy. Keywords: livestock management software, cattle analytics, precision agriculture, agtech, beef producers, feedlot management, farm data analytics, animal health tracking, cattle marketing, SaaS, Dane Kuper, Dustin Balsley, Performance Beef, Cattle Krush, Zoetis, Builders VC, agricultural technology, livestock profitability, feed efficiency, herd management

column5: Created technology that unobtrusively checks a person’s health status at home, providing timely clinical notifications to caregivers and clinicians
column6: Casana is a pioneering startup in the healthcare sector, focusing on in-home health monitoring through an innovative product called the Heart Seat. This smart toilet seat is designed to make health monitoring effortless and integrated into daily life. By simply sitting on the Heart Seat, users can have their health data securely recorded and uploaded to the Cloud via WiFi, with no extra effort required from them. The Heart Seat uses clinical algorithms to analyze health measurements and a proprietary bio-identification algorithm to ensure that only the prescribed user's data is displayed to the appropriate healthcare provider or team. This technology is particularly beneficial for individuals with chronic conditions such as hypertension, as it allows for continuous and accurate monitoring without the need for frequent doctor visits.Casana primarily serves patients, caregivers, and healthcare providers. The business model revolves around selling the Heart Seat to consumers and healthcare institutions, and potentially offering subscription services for data analysis and cloud storage.Revenue is generated through the sale of the Heart Seat units and possibly through ongoing service fees for data management and analysis. column5: Helps teams create powerful mobile products directly from their mobile device
column6: Play, founded in 2019 by Dan LaCivita, Eric Eng, Joon-Yong Park, and Michael Ferdman, is a New York-based company that has developed a design and prototyping platform specifically for creating mobile products on iOS. The company addresses a key challenge in the product design workflow where designers traditionally create mobile products on desktops and then use mirroring apps to simulate the experience on a phone, creating a disconnect between the design and the final product. Play's core offering is a native iOS design tool that allows users to design, prototype, and collaborate directly from their iPhone, iPad, or macOS device. This enables designers to experience their creations in real-time on the intended medium. The platform leverages native iOS features, allowing the incorporation of elements like live maps, pickers, modals, and the camera directly into the design process. It supports the import of designs from Figma, maintaining properties like Auto Layout, and allows for the creation of sophisticated interactions and gesture-based controls. A key feature is the ability to generate SwiftUI code from the designs in real-time, streamlining the handoff process from design to engineering. Prototypes can be shared seamlessly using Apple App Clips, eliminating the need for app installations for testers. The company operates on a subscription-based business model, offering different tiers such as Free, Pro, and Enterprise plans. These plans vary by features, including the number of projects, asset storage, and access to advanced functionalities like design system imports. Play has successfully raised a total of $14.6 million in funding over multiple rounds, with a significant Series A round of $9.1 million in November 2021 led by First Round Capital and Oceans. This financial backing supports the company's vision to bridge the gap between design and engineering, making it faster for teams to move from an idea to a functional, high-quality mobile application. Keywords: mobile app design, iOS design tool, native prototyping, SwiftUI code generation, Figma import, UI/UX design, interaction design, app development, product design platform, collaborative design, prototype sharing, App Clips, Apple Design Award, Dan LaCivita, Joon-Yong Park, First Round Capital, mobile-first design, design to code, native iOS features, high-fidelity prototyping, user testing

column6: Carnegie Foundry is a robotics and AI venture studio based in Pittsburgh, PA, that specializes in creating, funding, and growing companies focused on industrial automation. The firm partners with the National Robotics Engineering Center (NREC), a world-renowned authority in autonomous robotics and AI, to develop and commercialize solutions for various industries.The business model of Carnegie Foundry is that of a "venture founder," meaning they are involved in the entire lifecycle of a startup, from ideation and scoping to launching, seeding, staffing, and management. They deploy their own capital to bridge the funding gap often faced by hardware-driven, industrial automation startups. The firm focuses on verticals that are ripe for disruption through automation, such as the supply chain and logistics sectors.Carnegie Foundry makes money by taking equity in the companies it helps create and grow. As these startups mature and achieve commercial success, the value of Carnegie Foundry's stake in them increases. The firm has also launched Carnegie Capital Partners to accelerate early-stage investment in robotics and Industry 4.0, further solidifying its position in the market. column5: Virtual reality remote desktop app
column6: Bigscreen, founded in 2014 by CEO Darshan Shankar, operates at the intersection of social virtual reality and hardware. Shankar, a University of California, Berkeley graduate in Electrical Engineering & Computer Science and a Y Combinator alum, previously founded Flotype, a real-time communications technology company. This background in software engineering and entrepreneurship provided the foundation for Bigscreen's initial focus on creating shared virtual spaces. The company is backed by venture capital from firms including Andreessen Horowitz and True Ventures, having raised $14 million over two rounds, with the latest being a Series A in October 2017.The company's core business revolves around a multi-faceted VR platform that serves a global user base of over six million people. The initial offering is a social VR application that allows users to stream their computer desktop into a virtual environment, enabling them to play PC games, watch movies, and collaborate with others in shared virtual rooms. This software is available on major VR platforms like Meta Quest and SteamVR, supporting a wide range of headsets. Revenue for the software side is generated through the rental of 3D movies, facilitated by partnerships with major studios like Paramount Pictures.In a significant strategic evolution, Bigscreen expanded from software into hardware with the launch of the Bigscreen Beyond headset in 2023. This move was driven by the desire to create hardware optimized for the Bigscreen software experience, addressing comfort and immersion issues prevalent in the market. The Bigscreen Beyond is a high-end, wired PC VR headset known for being exceptionally lightweight at just 127 grams. Its key feature is its custom-built nature; each unit is manufactured to fit the specific user's face shape and interpupillary distance (IPD), based on a 3D scan provided during purchase. This bespoke design aims to eliminate light leakage and maximize comfort for long sessions. The headset features high-resolution micro-OLED displays (2560x2560 pixels per eye), pancake optics for improved clarity, and relies on SteamVR for tracking, requiring external base stations and controllers which are sold separately.Keywords: virtual reality, social VR, PC VR, VR headset, Bigscreen Beyond, Darshan Shankar, custom VR headset, micro-OLED, pancake lenses, SteamVR tracking, virtual movie theater, desktop streaming, collaborative VR, remote work, VR gaming, Andreessen Horowitz, True Ventures, VR hardware, lightweight headset, face scan customization

column5: AMMOÂ² helps gun owners stay stocked up on ammunition... automatically
column6: AmmoSquared Inc. operates a subscription-based service for ammunition, positioning itself as a digital solution for firearms owners. The company was conceived in 2013 and officially started in 2015 by the husband-and-wife team Dan and Danielle Morton. The founding idea, sparked by the ammunition shortages of 2013, was to create a service that allows gun owners to build up their ammunition inventory steadily over time, much like a 401k savings plan. Dan Morton, an avid shooter, experienced the market challenges firsthand and envisioned a "set it and forget it" system to avoid future supply disruptions. This concept evolved from a small operation in the Mortons' garage into a business that has generated millions in revenue and now serves over 80,000 members. The core of AmmoSquared's business is a recurring subscription model where customers allocate a budget, starting from as little as $5 per month, towards purchasing ammunition. This ammunition accumulates in the customer's digital inventory, which corresponds to physical rounds held in the company's secure, climate-controlled warehouses. Customers can manage their holdings online, choosing from over 75 caliber variations. They can set triggers for automatic shipment based on round count, time, or value, or request on-demand delivery. This model provides convenience and helps users budget their purchases while building a stockpile. Revenue is primarily generated from these ammunition sales. The company's platform treats ammunition like a commodity, allowing for the accumulation of fractional rounds and giving customers flexibility without needing to select specific brands for common practice rounds. This approach enables AmmoSquared to make bulk purchases from various manufacturers, which can lead to more competitive pricing. While this is a benefit for general stockpiling, customers seeking very specific loads for hunting or competition might find it less suitable. In 2019, the company incorporated and brought on Chris Corriveau, co-founder of the investor social network StockTwits, as CTO and Clay Knight as Head of Design to further develop the platform. The team's long-term vision is to create a digital ammunition exchange where users can not only purchase and store but also sell and trade ammunition, creating a market-based mechanism to help stabilize prices and availability. Keywords: ammunition subscription, ammo bank, digital ammo reserve, ammo stockpiling, online ammunition management, firearm owners, recurring ammo purchase, secure ammo storage, ammunition delivery, gun community, Dan Morton, Chris Corriveau, ammo exchange, caliber inventory, shooting sports, personal defense, ammo market, firearms industry, ammunition investing, bulk ammo purchasing, gun ownership solutions

column5: Self-healing concrete for sustainable and durable infrastructure.
column6: Mimicrete, founded in 2021, is a materials science company that originated from research at the University of Cambridge's Department of Engineering. The company was established by female founders Dr. Zijing (Liz) Li, Dr. Lívia Ribeiro de Souza, and Arta Selmani to address the fundamental problem of cracking in concrete, which leads to costly maintenance and significant carbon emissions. Dr. Li's doctoral research into biomimetic materials and vascular self-healing concrete forms the core of Mimicrete's technology. The firm is tackling the global concrete market, a sector responsible for up to 8% of annual carbon emissions, with a novel self-healing technology. The technology embeds a biomimetic vascular network within pre-fabricated concrete elements. This network, inspired by biological systems, contains a healing agent that is autonomously released when cracks occur. Unlike other solutions, this system can heal larger cracks (over 500μm) rapidly, often within 24 hours, and can perform multiple healing processes throughout the structure's lifetime. This process extends the lifespan of concrete structures, reduces the need for manual repairs, and can cut repair costs and associated carbon emissions by up to 50%. Mimicrete's business model appears flexible, including options for licensing its technology to manufacturers for a per-unit fee or royalty. The solution is designed as a 'drop-in' for existing precast manufacturing processes, requiring no significant changes to the concrete mix or production lines. The company targets the construction and infrastructure sectors, working with major infrastructure firms in the UK and Europe. It has secured funding through various channels, including an Innovate UK Smart Grant and investments from US-based venture capital firms like Leonas Capital, as well as participating in accelerator programs. Keywords: self-healing concrete, advanced materials, construction technology, sustainable construction, biomimetic, smart concrete, infrastructure maintenance, carbon reduction, civil engineering, concrete durability, vascular networks, precast concrete, cleantech, deep tech, materials science, concrete admixture, structural engineering, autonomous repair, Cambridge University spin-out, crack mitigation, lifecycle extension

column5: Owl AI designs, develops and sells 3D High Definition Thermal Night Vision sensor and camera solutions
column6: Owl Autonomous Imaging, established in 2018 by co-founders Chuck Gershman and Gene Petilli, is a specialized developer of thermal imaging and ranging solutions aimed at the automotive and industrial mobility sectors. The company is headquartered in Fairport, New York, with additional presence in Silicon Valley.The founders' backgrounds are deeply rooted in imaging and semiconductor technology. CEO Chuck Gershman brings over 30 years of experience in executive management within the tech and semiconductor industries, having led successful exits with acquisitions by Intel and PMC-Sierra. CTO Gene Petilli has an extensive career in technology, starting as an Analog ASIC Design Engineer at Eastman Kodak in 1985 and later serving as a director at Intrinsix, where he specialized in advanced imaging. The core technology behind Owl AI was initially adapted from a solution developed for the U.S. Air Force to track ballistic missiles, which the founders repurposed for the automotive market.Owl AI's primary business involves designing, developing, and selling 3D High-Definition Thermal Night Vision sensors and camera solutions to automotive Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs) and their Tier 1 suppliers. The company operates on a fabless semiconductor business model. Its main offering is the "Thermal Ranging" platform, a monocular 3D thermal camera that provides high-definition thermal video coupled with high-precision ranging. This system is designed to operate effectively day or night and in adverse weather conditions like fog and snow, where traditional visual cameras and even LiDAR can be compromised.The technology functions by passively detecting radiated heat signatures from objects. It then uses proprietary AI and deep learning algorithms, specifically convolutional neural networks (CNNs), to analyze the monochrome thermal images, extract dense 3D range maps, and classify objects. This enables the system to identify pedestrians, animals, cyclists, and other vehicles, calculating their position, direction, and speed to facilitate safer autonomous and semi-autonomous vehicle operation. A key differentiator is its ability to generate accurate depth perception and a 3D point cloud from a single 2D thermal camera, a capability the company calls monocular thermal ranging. The system can detect warm objects up to 200 yards away.The company has secured significant funding, including a $15 million Series A round in January 2022, led by State Farm Ventures, bringing its total raised to over $15.1 million across several rounds. This capital supports the development of cost-effective, high-definition thermal sensors for mass-market automotive adoption. Beyond automotive, Owl AI's technology has applications in industrial, construction, agriculture, defense, and smart infrastructure markets.Keywords: thermal imaging, autonomous vehicles, ADAS, computer vision, pedestrian detection, automotive safety, 3D sensing, sensor fusion, thermal ranging, machine learning

column5: Mywater – Be Smart. Drink Smart
column6: MyWater, a company operating in Pakistan, provides smart water purification systems designed to offer an alternative to bottled water. Founded in 2017 by Ammar Jangbarwala, with research and development beginning in 2016, the company officially launched its services in 2020. The current CEO is Shahrukh Nadeem, who brings a background in human capital management, marketing, and product strategy to the role. The company is headquartered in Karachi and has expanded its operations to Lahore, Islamabad, and Rawalpindi, with plans for further national expansion and a launch in the UAE. MyWater's business model is centered on a monthly subscription service, positioning water as a service rather than a product. For a fixed monthly fee, customers receive a smart water purifier, and the subscription covers all general servicing, parts replacement, and periodic filter changes. This model aims to provide a cost-effective solution, potentially reducing monthly water expenses by up to 70% compared to bottled water. The company targets both residential and corporate clients, including homes, offices, and institutions, with various models tailored to different needs, such as the compact 'NEO' for kitchens and the larger 'SOLACE' for corporate use. The core product is a line of smart water purifiers that connect to a user's tap water supply. These units employ a multi-stage filtration process that includes dual particle filtration, reverse osmosis, dual carbon filtration, nano-silver disinfection, UV disinfection, and mineralization to ensure water quality. A key feature is the elimination of plastic bottles, addressing health and environmental concerns associated with plastic usage. The systems are IoT-enabled, allowing users to monitor water quality, filter life, and consumption through a mobile app. This app also facilitates service requests and billing management. MyWater offers different water types, including still, sparkling, and alkaline options. Keywords: water purification, subscription service, smart home technology, drinking water, water-as-a-service, IoT, environmental sustainability, Pakistan, corporate solutions, residential services

column5: Revolutionize retail operations using artificial intelligence to place fresh food orders, avoiding spoilage due to overstocking or lost sales due to under stocking
column6: Aravita is a forward-thinking startup dedicated to revolutionizing the fresh food retail sector. The company addresses a significant issue highlighted by the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO), which estimates that 45% of all fruits and vegetables produced globally are wasted. Aravita's mission is to reduce this waste and enhance profitability for grocers through the use of advanced technology.Aravita operates in the fresh food retail market, serving grocery stores and supermarkets. The company leverages artificial intelligence (AI) to optimize fresh food purchase orders. By using AI, Aravita helps retailers reduce overstocking, which leads to food waste, and avoid unnecessary costs. Additionally, their intelligent forecasting and order recommendations ensure that stores minimize lost sales due to stale products or out-of-stock items.The business model of Aravita is centered around providing a technology solution that integrates seamlessly with the existing systems of grocery retailers. This technology not only helps in managing inventory more efficiently but also boosts sales by ensuring that fresh products are always available and in optimal condition. Retailers benefit from increased profitability and reduced waste, which also has positive social and environmental impacts.Aravita makes money by offering its AI-driven software as a service (SaaS) to grocery retailers. Clients pay a subscription fee for access to the platform, which provides continuous updates and improvements to the forecasting and inventory management algorithms. This model ensures a steady revenue stream for Aravita while delivering ongoing value to its clients.In summary, Aravita is a company that uses artificial intelligence to help grocery retailers optimize their fresh food operations, reduce waste, and increase profitability. By addressing both economic and environmental concerns, Aravita is positioned as a key player in the fresh food retail market.Keywords: AI, fresh food, retail, grocery, inventory management, waste reduction, profitability, forecasting, SaaS, technology.

column5: A fabless substrate vendor developing advanced packaging technology that bridges the gap between the slowing of Moore's Law and the rising demand for compute performance
column6: Chipletz operates as a fabless substrate vendor, concentrating on advanced packaging technology to address the performance demands of modern computing in the face of a decelerating Moore's Law. The company was established in 2021, originating as an internal venture within AMD in 2016 before spinning off. This strategic move positions Chipletz to leverage its architectural engineering know-how and established network of major customers. The founding team is composed of seasoned professionals from the semiconductor industry, many of whom are veterans of AMD. Co-founder and CEO Bryan Black, who previously served as a Senior Fellow at AMD, brings extensive experience in chip packaging from his time at both Intel and AMD. His background includes leading the development of multi-chip modules and the first implementation of high-bandwidth memory (HBM) in a GPU. The leadership also includes co-founders Michael Su (CTO), Michael Alfano, and MeeLan Lee. Michael Su, also an AMD veteran, is responsible for R&D and product development, with deep expertise in semiconductor interconnects and advanced packaging. Michael Alfano serves as the Vice President of Product Engineering. The firm's business model is centered on being a fabless substrate designer, developing and selling its proprietary "Smart Substrate™" technology. This product enables the heterogeneous integration of multiple integrated circuits (ICs), or chiplets, from potentially different manufacturers into a single package. By doing so, it aims to deliver superior die-to-die interconnects, faster external I/O, and improved power integrity compared to traditional multi-chip modules. This approach allows clients to scale system performance for demanding applications. Chipletz targets clients in high-growth sectors such as artificial intelligence, high-performance computing (HPC), and immersive consumer experiences. The company generates revenue by providing these advanced packaging solutions to chip designers and system builders. In September 2023, Chipletz secured an undisclosed amount in a Series B funding round, with SKC acquiring a significant stake. This investment highlights a strategic partnership aimed at combining SKC's glass substrate manufacturing capacity with Chipletz's packaging engineering expertise. The core offering, Smart Substrate™, functions as an active interposer, facilitating die-to-die communication and supporting multiple voltage domains from a single supply. A key benefit is the ability to integrate diverse dies, such as CPUs, GPUs, and HBM, from any vendor onto a single platform, effectively turning the substrate into the system itself. This modularity addresses the escalating costs and complexity of advanced semiconductor manufacturing by allowing different functional blocks to be produced using optimal process nodes before being assembled. In a demonstration, the technology successfully reduced the required metal layers from 16 to nine compared to a conventional silicon interposer design. Chipletz collaborates with EDA partners like Siemens to design and validate its complex, high-density packages, ensuring performance for very large designs with millions of connections. Keywords: advanced packaging, fabless substrate vendor, chiplet integration, Smart Substrate, system-in-package, heterogeneous integration, Moore's Law, high-performance computing, artificial intelligence workloads, die-to-die interconnect, semiconductor packaging, AMD spin-off, Bryan Black, Michael Su, multi-chip module, 2.5D packaging, 3D packaging, semiconductor substrates, Series B funding, SKC, high-bandwidth memory, silicon interposer alternative
