column5: Zorus - Cyber Security for the Channel
column6: Zorus Inc., established in 2017 by Greg Gage, operates as a cybersecurity provider with a strategic focus on the Managed Service Provider (MSP) channel. The company's genesis is deeply rooted in the leadership's prior experience at Datto, a prominent data protection and IT management solutions provider. Gage, previously the Director of Research and Development at Datto, leveraged his extensive knowledge of the IT channel to build Zorus. He was joined by other Datto veterans, including Najaf 'Naj' Raza as COO and Ian McChord, who later took on the CEO role, allowing Gage to focus on technology as CTO. This collective background instilled a deep understanding of the MSP market's unique needs and fostered a channel-only business model. The company's core offering is a suite of cybersecurity products designed to be sold and managed by MSPs for their small and mid-sized business clients. Revenue is generated through a tiered pricing model based on the number of endpoint licenses, allowing MSPs to bundle the service and create their own margin. Zorus's flagship product, Archon, is an endpoint-level web filtering and security platform. Unlike traditional DNS filters that block entire domains, Archon operates as an endpoint proxy, providing more granular control to isolate and block specific threats like malware, ransomware, and phishing scams without disrupting access to safe parts of a domain. This device-level agent ensures consistent protection for remote and in-office workers alike, a critical feature for the modern hybrid workforce. The platform also provides user behavior analytics and detailed reporting that MSPs can white-label to demonstrate value to their clients. A significant milestone in the company's history was securing a $9 million Series A funding round in December 2021, led by Asymmetric Capital Partners, which was aimed at expanding its detection and response capabilities. The company continued its growth trajectory and was ultimately acquired by DNSFilter in April 2025. This strategic acquisition was intended to combine DNSFilter's AI-driven threat intelligence with Zorus's endpoint filtering technology and deep expertise in the MSP market, creating a more comprehensive security solution. The integration aims to run Zorus's filtering capabilities on DNSFilter's infrastructure, enhancing performance and unifying the management experience for MSPs. Keywords: endpoint security, web filtering, cybersecurity for MSPs, channel-only security, DNS filtering, user behavior analytics, threat intelligence, remote workforce security, endpoint protection, cybersecurity platform, SaaS security, malware protection, phishing prevention, ransomware defense, MSP tools, IT channel, security as a service, Greg Gage, DNSFilter acquisition, Archon

column5: Stasys Medical is developing a patient focused test method for analyzing blood clotting disorders and platelet dysfunction
column6: Stasys Medical Corporation, headquartered in Seattle, Washington, is a medical diagnostics firm focused on developing solutions for managing blood coagulation issues. The company was incorporated in Washington on February 27, 2017. Its core mission is to create a system that can quickly identify deficiencies in a patient's ability to clot blood, delivering critical information to healthcare providers in emergency situations. The company targets the significant challenge of managing bleeding in trauma patients, where rapid and accurate diagnostics can be life-saving.The development of Stasys Medical's technology is rooted in research conducted at the University of Washington. The company has secured funding through various channels to advance its product development, including a notable $3.5 million seed round in 2020, followed by an additional $2.2 million. Further financial backing was obtained through a Series A financing round in 2023, which raised $9.5 million. These funding rounds underscore investor confidence in the company's approach to hemostasis analysis.Stasys's key offering is a point-of-care diagnostic platform designed to provide a comprehensive picture of a patient's clotting status in under 10 minutes. This system analyzes a small blood sample to assess clot formation, firmness, and fibrinolysis, which is the process of clot breakdown. By delivering a faster and more complete analysis than traditional lab tests, the platform aims to empower clinicians to make more informed and timely decisions regarding blood product transfusions and other interventions. The business model centers on the sale of these diagnostic devices and associated consumables to hospitals and emergency care facilities. The system is currently in the investigational use phase and has not yet been cleared by the FDA. Keywords: medical diagnostics, hemostasis, coagulation testing, point-of-care, trauma care, blood analysis, fibrinolysis, medical device, clinical diagnostics, emergency medicine

column5: Social Communities In Real Life
column6: LocalX (localx.org) offers a specialized consultancy focused on building chapter-based communities for businesses. The firm leverages the methodology and experience gained from the global #LinkedInLocal movement. This movement began in June 2017 when co-founder Anna McAfee posted on LinkedIn with a simple idea: to meet her online connections in her local town of Coffs Harbour, Australia. This initial event sparked a worldwide phenomenon. Within a month, with the help of early adopters Alexandra Galviz in London, Manu Goswami in New York City, and Erik Eklund in Brussels, #LinkedInLocal expanded to four global communities. Over the subsequent two years, this organic, grassroots movement grew to encompass over 1,000 communities in 100 countries, all run by local hosts for their own communities and centered on values of authenticity and collaboration. The movement demonstrated a significant desire for genuine, in-person connections among professionals.Following the success of this not-for-profit initiative, LocalX was established as a commercial venture to package and share these proven community-building strategies with other organizations. The company's primary service is guiding businesses on how to create and nurture their own localized, event-based communities. In an interview, McAfee mentioned the development of LocalX as a digital platform to enable brands and companies to build these communities, suggesting a model that combines consulting with a technology solution. The business targets companies and brands seeking to foster deeper engagement and build strong relationships with their customers or members by moving online interactions into real-world settings. The revenue model is based on providing this expertise and methodology to its clients.Keywords: community building, chapter-based communities, local events, corporate community, brand engagement, community strategy, offline engagement, professional networking, community consulting, event-based marketing, member engagement, #LinkedInLocal, Anna McAfee, customer community, community platform, local marketing, brand community, corporate networking, community management, professional events

column5: Engineering the future of precision metal materials
column6: Based in Rochester, NY, ZeroValent NanoMetals (ZVNM) is a specialty chemicals and materials science company established in 2016. The firm operates at the intersection of nanotechnology and additive manufacturing, developing and producing nanometal inks and corrosion-resistant coating materials. These products are designed for both 2D and 3D metal ink jetting applications.  ZVNM's core technology revolves around proprietary chemical stabilization processes that control the reactivity of transition metals like copper, nickel, iron, and zinc. The company has mastered the synthesis of stable, fully-reduced nanotransition metal colloidal dispersions, producing uniform metal particles in the challenging sub-3-nanometer size range. This capability allows for the creation of print media with distinct advantages over traditional powdered metal technologies. Key benefits include significantly lower sintering temperatures, which reduces power requirements and increases production throughput. The resulting products exhibit high resistance to oxidation, greater tensile strength, and electrical and thermal conductivity that approaches that of bulk metals.  The company serves clients in markets such as printable flexible electronics and additive manufacturing. For electronics manufacturers, ZVNM offers nanometal inks made from earth-abundant metals, providing a new level of economy and performance for printing conductive traces and devices. For additive manufacturing supply distributors, the company provides colloidal nano-metal products that create an incremental revenue opportunity. ZVNM also engages with business partners to create custom-tailored colloidal nanoparticle dispersions for specific applications, with specifications for metal concentration, particle size, viscosity, and surface tension. While ZVNM is focused on selling a range of standard products, it also collaborates with clients on special applications in metal coatings and additive manufacturing. The company's online store lists a family of products under the 'NuMetal' brand, including copper, nickel, and iron inks, although they are noted as being in a developmental stage.  Keywords: nanometal ink, specialty chemicals, additive manufacturing, 3D metal printing, colloidal dispersions, nanotechnology, conductive ink, flexible electronics, corrosion resistance, transition metals, low-temperature sintering, material science, nanometal synthesis, copper nanoparticles, nickel nanoparticles, zinc nanoparticles, custom material engineering, nanoparticle stabilization, printed electronics, advanced manufacturing

Rocourt, an award-winning cannabinoid formulator and co-founder of the cannabis wellness brand Papa & Barkley, developed natural solutions to help his wife, which proved to be life-changing. This personal success led them to establish CLEA Midlife to make these solutions accessible to more women. The company's business model is direct-to-consumer, with products sold through its website. CLEA Midlife targets women in midlife who are navigating the physical and emotional challenges of the menopausal transition. Their mission is twofold: to provide effective, plant-based products and to change the negative narrative surrounding menopause by offering educational content and fostering a sense of empowerment. The company emphasizes clean, chemical-free formulations using whole-plant hemp extracts with no fillers. All products contain a full spectrum of cannabinoids, including CBD, CBG, and CBN, with less than 0.3% THC, making them non-psychoactive. The product line includes capsules and body oils designed to address specific symptoms. The 'FOCUS' capsules contain CBG to improve mental clarity and reduce brain fog. The 'EASE' capsules feature CBD to help with anxiety and aches, while the 'SLEEP' capsules are formulated with CBD, CBN, and a micro-dose of THC to aid with insomnia. A 'NOURISH' CBD body oil is also available. The company has a medical advisor, Dr. Suzanne Gilberg-Lenz, an OB-GYN and author, to ensure the educational content is clear and evidence-informed. column5: Media company and competitive robot combat league
column6: Havoc Robotics is a media and sports company that operates the National Havoc Robot League (NHRL), the world's largest robot combat league. The company was founded in 2018 by Austin McChord, the founder of the data backup company Datto. McChord, a computer engineer and robotics hobbyist, started the league after leaving his day-to-day role at Datto. What began as a hobby with a single cage in an old building quickly grew into an international destination for robot combat enthusiasts. In 2021, Kelly Biderman, an executive with a background in media, joined as CEO to help scale the league and its content. NHRL organizes a series of tournaments throughout the year at its facility in Norwalk, Connecticut, which features multiple arenas, a robot repair pit, and a museum of past combat robots. Competitors, known as builders, design and construct remote-controlled robots in 3lb, 12lb, and 30lb weight classes to fight in head-to-head matches inside enclosed arenas. The events are open to builders of all skill levels, and registration is free to lower the barrier to entry. Winners receive cash prizes and an invitation to the annual World Championship. The company has committed to donating millions to STEM-focused charities, with funds awarded by tournament finalists to organizations of their choice. The company's business model is centered on content creation and distribution, sponsorships, and live events. Havoc Robotics produces high-quality digital content of the fights, which are streamed live on platforms like YouTube and later broadcast on networks such as Cheddar, reaching a fast-growing global audience. This content-first approach has resulted in significant audience growth, with hundreds of millions of views on social media. Revenue is generated through partnerships, sponsorships, and ticket sales for live events, which feature food trucks and other spectator-friendly activities. The company also offers educational resources through its Havoc Academy, which provides kits and courses for aspiring robot builders. column6: Onena Medicines AG is a Stanford University spin-out biotechnology company based in Switzerland that creates revolutionary AI-optimized medicines to deliver hope where it’s needed most. Onena´s growing pipeline of antibody programs targets a class of powerful secreted growth factors called Dual SMAD Inhibiting Proteins (DSIPs), which are needed for diseased cells to grow and mature. Neutralizing DSIPs has the potential to impact major problems in human health, ranging from cancer to obesity. Onena is the world´s first company to develop a computer vision-based system to design antibody medicines from scratch in silico.We have two main competitive advantages. First, our growing first-in-class pipeline targets a class of novel powerful secreted growth factors called Dual SMAD Inhibiting Proteins (DSIPs), which we discovered are necessary for diseased cells to grow and mature. Our lead program, OMED-1, has demonstrated proof-of-concept in vivo efficacy in breast, brain, and colorectal cancers already. Second, we are the only company in the world that is developing a computer vision-based generative AI antibody discovery platform (NVIDIA took notice last year).

column6: Smartbox Lockers (operating as Smartbox Ecommerce Solutions Pvt. Ltd. in India and Smartbox USA Inc. in the United States) is an original equipment manufacturer (OEM) of automated smart locker systems designed to streamline package delivery, pickup, storage, and asset management. Founded in 2015, the company builds both the hardware (indoor, outdoor, and refrigerated locker units with tamper-proof compartments, 24/7 surveillance, and modular configurations) and a cloud-based software platform that provides real-time tracking, access control via PIN or QR code, automated audit trails, and API integrations with third-party systems such as Yardi, Entrata, and ServiceNow.Smartbox serves a wide range of B2B verticals including residential property management, retail and e-commerce (click-and-collect / BOPIS), logistics and last-mile delivery, banking (self-service branch lockers), corporate offices, universities, and healthcare. Notable early deployments include India's first smart locker network in partnership with Delhi Metro Rail Corporation (DMRC) in 2016 and the first banking-sector locker network for ICICI Bank in 2017. The company expanded internationally starting in 2018–2019 with a deployment for The Commercial Bank of Qatar and reports over 14,000 lockers installed across more than 10 countries.The company positions itself as an industry-agnostic locker infrastructure provider, offering end-to-end solutions from hardware manufacturing through software management and ongoing support. column5: One handpicked dating match per day
column6: Once positions itself in the competitive online dating market by championing a "slow dating" methodology, a direct counterpoint to the rapid-swiping culture popularized by apps like Tinder. The company was founded in March 2015 by Jean Meyer, Guillaume Sempé, Guilhem Duche, and Fabrice Fardeau. Meyer, a serial entrepreneur with a background in computer science and an MBA from Columbia Business School, had previously co-founded the student dating site DateMySchool. This earlier venture's success was hampered by the rise of swipe-based apps, which motivated Meyer to create a new, differentiated dating experience. The core of Once's service is providing users with a single, algorithmically selected match each day at noon. This model is designed to encourage more thoughtful consideration of potential partners. The AI-powered algorithm analyzes user information, dating preferences, and interaction history to refine its selections. Users have 24 hours to interact with their daily match and decide whether to continue the conversation. A distinctive feature, introduced to enhance safety and accountability, allows women to rate men they have dated in real life based on conversation quality and profile accuracy. The business generates revenue through a freemium model. While basic matching is free, users can subscribe to a VIP service or make in-app purchases for premium features. These paid options are designed to increase a user's chances of finding a successful match. In January 2021, Once was acquired by Dating Group for approximately $18 million in a deal comprising cash and stock. At the time of the acquisition, the app had over 10 million users, primarily in Western Europe, and had established a profitable business. Following the acquisition, co-founder Clémentine Lalande, who had previously founded the dating app Pickable, continued to lead the company as CEO, with Alexandra Beaumont taking over leadership in 2021. Keywords: slow dating, online dating, dating app, matchmaking, freemium, AI algorithm, relationship-focused, Jean Meyer, Clémentine Lalande, Dating Group, quality over quantity, daily match, user ratings, in-app purchases, subscription model, mobile dating, European market, meaningful connections, dating technology, user safety

column5: The immediately productive, shareable cloud database
column6: Bit.io was a database software company established in 2019 and headquartered in San Francisco. The firm was founded by Adam Fletcher, who serves as CEO, and Dr. Jonathan Mortensen, the CTO. Fletcher's background includes leadership roles in engineering at Google, working on production networking and YouTube, while Mortensen is a data scientist from Stanford who has led data science teams in the medical and cybersecurity fields. The founders' direct experience with the operational difficulties of data collaboration inspired them to create a platform that simplifies data sharing and teamwork.  The company developed a shareable, serverless cloud database platform centered around PostgreSQL. Its core mission was to enable developers and data analysts to become immediately productive with data by eliminating complex configuration and setup processes. The platform targeted a range of clients, from individual developers and startups to large enterprises like Ford, Visa, and Morgan Stanley, for uses including web application development, data analysis, and low-code backends. The business model was based on usage, with premium offerings for individuals and teams based on the number of rows queried after a generous free tier.  Bit.io's main offering was a zero-config, serverless Postgres database that could be created with a single click or by dragging and dropping a data file (like CSV, XLS, or JSON). Key features included a web-based SQL editor, one-click sharing with collaborators, automatic scaling of resources based on workload, and broad compatibility with any tool that supports Postgres. The platform's design was to make creating and sharing a database as straightforward as sharing a Google Doc. In October 2022, the company announced it had raised $7.5 million in a seed funding round led by Battery Ventures and GreatPoint Ventures. By this time, it had grown to over 15,000 users who had created 30,000 databases. On May 1, 2023, Bit.io was acquired by Databricks, and subsequently, the bit.io product was sunsetted, with the team joining Databricks to work on the developer experience for its Lakehouse platform. column5: Complete solution for measuring blast exposure
column6: BlackBox Biometrics, established in 2011, is a technology firm that develops sensor systems to monitor and analyze blast and impact forces, which are often linked to traumatic brain injuries. The company originated from a research project at the Rochester Institute of Technology's Venture Creations business incubator, funded by a DARPA grant, to address the need for objective data on blast-related injuries among U.S. service members. The founder, David Borkholder, a professor of microsystems engineering at RIT, led the development of the company's foundational technology. BlackBox Biometrics graduated from the incubator in 2014 and was acquired by AirBoss Defense Group (ADG) in May 2021 for a total potential consideration of up to $27 million. The company's primary business involves the sale of its proprietary sensor systems and accompanying software to a diverse client base that includes the U.S. Department of Defense, international militaries, law enforcement agencies, and athletic organizations. Its revenue is generated through the sale of hardware and the licensing of its data analysis software. The acquisition by ADG has allowed the firm to expand its market reach internationally and into non-military applications. BlackBox Biometrics offers two main product lines. The first, the Blast Gauge® System, is a set of three wearable sensors for the head, shoulder, and chest that record pressure and acceleration from explosive events. This system provides real-time data via wireless transmission or visual LED indicators (green, yellow, red) to facilitate immediate triage and post-event analysis by medical personnel. It has been deployed with U.S. Special Forces, various branches of the U.S. Military, and over 100 law enforcement agencies. The second product, the Linx Impact Assessment System (Linx IAS®), is tailored for the sports market. This lightweight sensor, worn in a headband or skullcap, measures head impacts and transmits data via Bluetooth to a mobile app, allowing coaches, parents, and trainers to monitor athletes for potential concussion-causing events in real-time. The Linx IAS® can sync over 128 players to a single device and was recognized as a CES Innovation Awards Honoree in 2015. Keywords: blast overpressure sensors, traumatic brain injury monitoring, impact assessment systems, wearable sensor technology, defense technology, athlete safety, concussion detection, military sensor technology, law enforcement equipment, David Borkholder, AirBoss Defense Group, Blast Gauge System, Linx IAS, impact data analysis, real-time feedback, TBI research, survivability solutions, tactical gear, sports technology, head impact monitoring, blast dosimetry, human performance optimization

column5: Technology firm assisting researchers and corporations in advancing their technologies to the market
column6: First Wave Technologies, Inc. is a medical device company specializing in advanced anesthesia systems designed to improve patient safety and reduce healthcare costs. Operating primarily in the medical technology sector, the company serves hospitals and healthcare facilities worldwide. Its flagship product, the Clarity IA system, converts existing ventilators into high-end anesthesia machines without requiring significant capital investment. This system is intended for patients requiring inhalation anesthesia and sedation during mechanical ventilation, offering a safer and more efficient alternative to traditional IV sedation. The business model revolves around selling disposable components that isolate airflow between patients and ventilators, thereby preventing cross-contamination and enhancing patient safety. First Wave Technologies generates revenue through the sale of these disposable components and licensing its patented technology. The company holds a portfolio of 58 patents and applications, ensuring a competitive edge in the market. By focusing on reducing the incidence of delirium and other complications associated with prolonged mechanical ventilation, First Wave Technologies aims to minimize ICU costs and improve patient outcomes. column5: Innovative product development and engineering services
column6: C Speed, LLC operates as a specialized original equipment manufacturer (OEM) in the defense and civilian surveillance markets, focusing on the engineering and delivery of advanced radar systems and subsystems. Founded in 2006 by David Lysack and headquartered in Liverpool, New York, the company leverages a team with over three decades of experience in the radar industry. A significant milestone occurred in October 2024 when C Speed was acquired by NewSpring Holdings, integrating it into NewSpring's Federal Platform, a strategic move designed to accelerate growth and expand its footprint in the government services sector. Following the acquisition, the company appointed Kevin McLaughlin as CEO. The company's business model is centered on designing, prototyping, and manufacturing state-of-the-art, American-made radar solutions. It serves a high-profile client base that includes prime defense contractors and government agencies such as the U.S. Air Force, Navy, Department of Homeland Security (DHS), Customs and Border Protection (CBP), and NATO. Revenue is generated through direct sales of its systems, engineering services, and long-term contracts for operations and sustainment, such as its involvement in the Tethered Aerostat Radar System (TARS) program. At the core of C Speed's product line is the LightWave™ Software-Defined Radar (LWR) platform. This modular, software-first architecture is the foundation for all its radar solutions, enabling rapid reconfiguration for various mission requirements across airborne and ground-based domains. A key product is the LightWave S-Band Solid-State Primary Surveillance Radar, a flexible 360° air surveillance platform. This system is notable for its ability to operate in different modes, making it effective for missions like gap-filler radar, wind turbine radar interference mitigation (WTRIM), and detecting low-observable targets in high-clutter environments. The company's offerings also include comprehensive design engineering services and the modernization of legacy radar systems, which enhances performance and extends service life while aiming to reduce total cost of ownership. column5: AI-powered platform for streamlined small business loans
column6: Levr.ai is a Vancouver-based financial technology company developing an intelligent loan platform to streamline access to capital for small and medium-sized businesses (SMBs). Founded in 2021 by CEO Kaylan Pepin and CTO Roman Hartmann, the company aims to simplify the complex process of securing business financing. Pepin, a Chartered Professional Accountant, brings years of experience in financing technology startups at major Canadian banks and identified the challenges qualified businesses face in obtaining and managing loans. Hartmann has a background in data, AI, and machine learning, and is focused on providing customers with ML-driven insights. The company's core product is an AI-powered marketplace that connects SMBs with over 50 lenders in Canada and the United States. Businesses can connect their banking and accounting software to the platform, which then uses AI and machine learning algorithms to analyze their financial data and match them with suitable loan options. The platform is designed to be free for businesses, generating revenue by charging a commission to the lender when a loan is successfully funded. Levr.ai also offers a white-label loan intake tool for lenders and a platform for brokers to manage the entire loan process in one place, from lender matching to commission tracking. Levr.ai has seen significant growth, supporting over 2,000 small businesses in North America and processing substantial loan request values. The company has raised a total of $2.5 million in seed funding over multiple rounds, with investors including Weave VC, Sprout VC, and Mavan Capital Partners. This funding is intended to expand its reach and enhance the platform's capabilities across North America. column5: Marine-based technology removing CO₂ from oceans to combat climate change and restore marine ecosystems
column6: Ephemeral Carbon is a startup based in Santa Clara, USA, focused on creating marine carbon dioxide removal (mCDR) solutions to fight climate change. With a strong emphasis on sustainability and climate impact, they collaborate globally to pilot and scale these solutions. Although still early-stage, their innovative approach positions them as a promising leader in ocean-based carbon removal efforts. column6: Shimmur was conceptualized in 2014 by Matthew Peltier, initially as a mobile application designed to foster interaction between social media influencers and their fanbases. The platform operated on a model similar to Reddit, where fans could create content within dedicated groups, or "Tribes," centered around their favorite online personalities, with the most popular content being upvoted. The core idea emerged from Peltier's realization of the disconnect between high-profile influencers and their large followings, aiming to create a more scalable and direct engagement channel.The business model intended to generate revenue through a split on sales facilitated by influencers on the platform. For instance, an influencer could post a link to a product, and Shimmur would receive a percentage of the sales generated from that link. In its early stages, the company gained traction, reporting over 166,000 registered users without any paid marketing, relying on organic growth as influencers brought their fans to the app. Shimmur successfully raised approximately $7 million from investors including Greycroft, Arena Ventures, and Techstars.Despite the initial user growth and funding, the business model failed to gain significant momentum. By 2017, the company's social media presence ceased, and in early 2018, the Shimmur website began redirecting to a new entity. This marked a significant pivot for the company. With the involvement of co-founder Josh Rosenheck and new investment from notable figures like Ashton Kutcher and Guy Oseary, Shimmur was recapitalized and rebranded into what is now known as Community. The new entity shifted focus from a tribe-based app to a direct-to-fan text messaging service, charging public figures and brands monthly or annual fees for a direct communication line to their audience, free from advertisements and social media algorithms.Keywords: Shimmur, Matthew Peltier, influencer engagement, fan community, social media platform, creator economy, Reddit-style app, direct-to-fan, Community.com, Ashton Kutcher, Guy Oseary, venture capital, tech pivot, text marketing, SMS platform, fan interaction, content creator tools, social commerce

column5: HyPoint, Inc. is developing the next generation hydrogen fuel cell system for air transport
column6: Hypoint is a Silicon Valley-based company specializing in the development of advanced hydrogen fuel cell systems for the aviation industry. The company targets aircraft manufacturers and aerospace companies looking for sustainable and efficient energy solutions. Hypoint operates in the renewable energy and aerospace markets, focusing on zero-emission air transportation. The business model revolves around the design, production, and sale of high-performance hydrogen fuel cells, which are crucial for reducing greenhouse gas emissions in aviation. Revenue is generated through the sale of these fuel cell systems and associated services. Hypoint's core innovation lies in its turbo air-cooled HTPEM (High-Temperature Proton Exchange Membrane) hydrogen fuel cell system, which offers both high specific power and high energy density, addressing the limitations of traditional Li-ion batteries and hydrogen fuel cells. The company is backed by leading venture capital firms and individual investors, and it boasts state-of-the-art R&D facilities and partnerships to support its mission. Keywords: hydrogen fuel cells, zero-emission aviation, turbo air-cooled, HTPEM, aerospace, renewable energy, high specific power, energy density, Silicon Valley, sustainable energy.

column5: Automated customer service software through the use of artificial intelligence
column6: Founded in 2012 by a team of academics and researchers from UC Berkeley, Wise.io emerged from a project applying machine learning to astrophysics. The founders—Joshua Bloom, Damian Eads, Dan Starr, Henrik Brink, and Joseph Richards—leveraged their expertise in statistics and computer science to address business challenges. Joshua Bloom, a Professor of Astrophysics at UC Berkeley, and his colleagues saw the potential to transition their advanced analytical frameworks from understanding celestial phenomena to solving real-world business problems. This led to the creation of a platform focused on making sophisticated machine learning accessible to enterprises. Damian Eads, another founder, brought a deep background in computer science, including a PhD specializing in machine learning for object detection and experience at Los Alamos National Lab. Wise.io operated as a B2B technology company, offering a machine learning platform designed to help businesses analyze large datasets to identify patterns and trends. Initially, the company's applications were aimed at optimizing the entire customer lifecycle, including acquisition, monetization, and retention. A key product was a predictive intelligence solution built to augment and automate customer support, enabling companies to handle more inquiries, decrease response times, and allow human agents to concentrate on more complex issues. The platform was designed for easy implementation and integration with common cloud-based business applications, such as those from Salesforce and Zendesk, making it accessible to users without deep technical expertise. Before its acquisition, Wise.io served a range of clients, from startups to Fortune 500 corporations, including notable names like Pinterest, Twilio, and Thumbtack. The company secured a total of $2.5 million in funding over two rounds. It began with a seed round on May 31, 2013, from Alchemist Accelerator and Startup Accelerator. This was followed by a Series A round on March 17, 2014, which raised $2.5 million, led by Voyager Capital. In November 2016, General Electric (GE) acquired Wise.io to enhance the machine learning and data science capabilities of its Predix platform, an operating system for the Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT). The acquisition was part of GE's strategy to bolster its offerings in areas like edge capabilities and machine learning. Wise.io's technology, particularly its unsupervised learning capabilities, became instrumental for GE in analyzing industrial sensor data and advancing its "Digital Twin" solutions, which are virtual models of industrial assets. Keywords: machine learning platform, predictive intelligence, customer support automation, industrial internet of things, IIoT, data science, GE Digital, Predix platform, big data analytics, customer experience management, intelligent automation, predictive applications, business process optimization, anomaly detection, digital twin, Alchemist Accelerator, Voyager Capital, Joshua Bloom, Damian Eads

column5: Custom development and managed services to education technology clients
column6: Founded in 2006 by Victoria Van Voorhis, an entrepreneur with over 17 years of experience in educational technology, Second Avenue Learning operates at the intersection of education and interactive media. Van Voorhis, who holds an undergraduate degree from Smith College and a master's from New York University, established the company to blend pedagogy with technology, creating engaging learning solutions. Her background as an educator and passion for gaming shaped the firm's core mission to develop immersive learning experiences with measurable student outcomes. Second Avenue Learning is a Rochester, NY-based certified women-owned business that was acquired by zSpace, Inc., a provider of augmented and virtual reality (AR/VR) educational solutions, on April 15, 2025. Prior to its acquisition, the company was venture-backed, with investors including American Public Education and the U.S. National Science Foundation. The firm specializes in custom software development, creating interactive modules and serious games for the education sector. Its business model revolves around providing bespoke development and managed services to a client base that includes prominent education technology companies like Pearson and McGraw-Hill, as well as K-12 schools, higher education institutions, and corporate markets. The company's revenue is generated through these custom service contracts and the licensing of its proprietary products. The company's services encompass product design, content creation, and software development. Their team structure is synergistic, comprising subject matter experts, programmers, artists, learning designers, and project managers to build pedagogically accurate and visually compelling educational tools. Proprietary products include "Election Edge," a game that immerses students in the U.S. presidential election process, and "Voices for Suffrage," an interactive platform with primary sources and games about the suffrage movement. The acquisition by zSpace is set to integrate Second Avenue Learning’s content creation expertise with zSpace's AR/VR platform, aiming to enhance offerings in K-12, higher education, and workforce development with more comprehensive, hands-on learning tools. Van Voorhis has transitioned to a product strategy leadership role at zSpace, continuing her work on delivering immersive educational experiences. Keywords: educational technology, serious games, interactive learning, custom software development, K-12 education, higher education, corporate training, learning management systems, EdTech, gamification, curriculum development, women-owned business, Victoria Van Voorhis, zSpace, immersive learning, digital education, content authoring, learning modules, education software, simulation based learning, virtual reality education, augmented reality learning

column5: AI-driven mental health navigation and self-therapy app
column6: Being Cares, Inc. is a health tech startup that developed Being, an AI-powered mobile application for mental wellness. Founded in 2020 by Varun Gandhi and Abhishek Sharma, the company is based in Bengaluru, India, and also incorporated in Delaware, US. The Being app is designed to help users map and address mental health concerns through an accessible interface. It provides personalized mini-therapy sessions, AI-powered journaling, and educational insights into psychological concepts. The application utilizes a combination of therapeutic techniques, including CBT, EMDR, and DBT, to address issues like anxiety, stress, depression, and burnout. Features include guided meditations, calming music, and interactive exercises. The app's AI, named PSY, offers personalized psycho-educational content to help users understand their issues. In July 2025, Being Cares was acquired by LISSUN, a mental health platform. This strategic acquisition merged LISSUN's network of therapists with Being Cares' AI technology to create a more comprehensive family-focused mental health ecosystem. Following the acquisition, Varun Gandhi joined LISSUN as Chief Product Officer and Abhishek Sharma as Chief Technology Officer. The business operates on a subscription model and is available on both iOS and Android platforms, with a free version also planned. Prior to its acquisition, Being Cares had received investments from firms such as Astir Ventures, Better Capital, and Multiply Ventures. Keywords: mental wellness, AI therapy, self-care app, cognitive behavioral therapy, CBT, mental health navigation, guided meditation, AI journaling, stress reduction, anxiety management, LISSUN, Varun Gandhi, Abhishek Sharma, personalized therapy, mobile health, health tech, emotional well-being, mindfulness, digital therapy, subscription model

column6: Questra World emerged claiming to be an advertising broker for an investment fund named Atlantic Global Asset Management (AGAM). The company, led by CEO José Manuel Gilabert, presented itself with Spanish management, despite its predecessor, Questra Holdings, having Russian roots. It promised investors exceptionally high weekly returns, sometimes up to 6.47%, by investing in activities like IPOs and corporate rehabilitations. The business model relied on a multi-level marketing (MLM) structure, where affiliates earned commissions by recruiting new investors. This structure, combined with the unusually high promised returns, quickly raised red flags. Financial regulators across Europe, including those in Belgium, Austria, Poland, and Spain, began issuing warnings against Questra and AGAM, stating they were not licensed to provide investment services and exhibited clear signs of a pyramid or Ponzi scheme. The significant event for Questra was not a funding round or an IPO, but a collapse. As regulatory pressure mounted in Europe, the company struggled to pay its promised returns, which relied on the inflow of new investment capital. In 2017, Questra World suspended affiliate ROI withdrawals, effectively imploding. The operation then attempted to reboot under new names like Five Winds Asset Management and Asia Trade Group to target investors in Asia, but the core issues remained. Key figures like Pavel Krymov were later arrested in connection with the scheme.

column5: Zero-knowledge proof authentication for zero-trust networks
column6: ZKX Solutions is a U.S.-based technology company that develops cyber defense technology for zero-trust networks. The company originated as a research and development effort within REDCOM Laboratories, Inc., a supplier of C2 solutions to the U.S. Military, and was officially launched as an independent business unit in May 2022. Collin Sweeney, a co-founder and co-inventor of the core technology, serves as the company's CTO and Chief Strategist. His background is in mathematics, cryptography, and cybersecurity. Sal Ceravolo, the Director of Product Development & Market Research, brings experience from REDCOM and the U.S. Army, where he supervised communications for the White House. The company's main product is ZKX Helix, a patented, next-generation multi-factor authentication (MFA) software platform. ZKX Helix is built upon zero-knowledge proofs, a cryptographic method that allows one party to prove to another that a statement is true, without revealing any information beyond the validity of the statement itself. This means no secrets like passwords or private keys are stored on servers or transmitted across the network, eliminating threats like data exfiltration and network infiltration from conventional attacks. The software handles user and device authentication simultaneously and is designed to be agnostic to existing devices, networks, transport, and storage mechanisms. ZKX Solutions primarily targets government, military, and commercial markets, with an initial focus on tactical and disconnected environments. Its technology serves as a foundational component for organizations migrating to a Zero Trust security architecture, which treats all network traffic as untrusted. The ZKX Helix platform enables precise, granular, and dynamic policy enforcement for access control, allowing organizations to control exactly what resources partners can access in merged network operations. The technology has been validated by third-party security firms and demonstrated in U.S. Army events like Cyber Quest. Keywords: zero-knowledge proof, multi-factor authentication, MFA, zero trust architecture, cybersecurity, access control, policy enforcement, network security, authentication engine, cyber defense, critical infrastructure protection, user and device authentication, tactical communications, government, military, cryptography, ZKP, REDCOM

column6: Smart Farm Systems Inc., established in 2013 by Bob Farinelli and Ryan Heringer, operates at the intersection of agriculture and technology from its headquarters in Nicholasville, Kentucky. The firm is positioned as a developer of farm monitoring systems, targeting a business-to-business (B2B) client base within the agricultural sector. Its core mission is to provide farmers with actionable, real-time data concerning their equipment and crops. The company's offerings are designed to enhance operational efficiency by enabling remote monitoring and control over crucial farm assets. Smart Farm Systems has developed a product suite that includes solutions for feed inventory monitoring, alarm management for livestock protection, and livestock performance tracking. A key technology is its proprietary 900 MHz wireless network, which allows farmers to oversee and analyze soil conditions and manage field equipment like irrigation pumps and grain silos without depending on external cellular or satellite services. This infrastructure is complemented by an analytics-based user interface. For feed mills, the company provides an integrated software package that handles ordering, point-of-sale, and accounts receivable, streamlining the entire process from order to billing and payment collection. Since its inception, the company has navigated several funding rounds to fuel its growth. It secured its first funding on February 18, 2015, and has since raised a total of $5.5 million over seven rounds, with the latest being a seed round in July 2021. This financial backing has been supported by institutional investors such as Keyhorse Capital, Mammoth View, and Village Capital. While one source indicates the company may be out of business, others list it as active. column6: Lantern Finance is a US-based company specializing in providing a straightforward staking platform for Ethereum (ETH) holders. The company aims to make it easy for everyday crypto investors to stake their ETH and earn rewards. Staking involves locking up a portion of ETH to participate in the validation process of the Ethereum blockchain, which helps maintain the network's security and efficiency. In return, stakers earn rewards in the form of additional ETH.Lantern Finance primarily serves individual crypto investors, ranging from beginners to seasoned experts. The company operates in the cryptocurrency market, specifically focusing on Ethereum staking. Its business model revolves around charging a 15% fee on the staking rewards earned by its users. This fee covers the costs associated with custody and running validators, which are essential for the staking process.The company ensures a seamless staking experience by offering a user-friendly platform that caters to all levels of crypto investors. Lantern Finance emphasizes security by partnering with BitGo, a leading US-based crypto custodian, to safeguard users' assets. Additionally, the platform is legally compliant and actively works to expand its services to more states.Lantern Finance generates revenue through the 15% fee on staking rewards. This fee structure allows users to keep a significant portion of their rewards while covering the operational costs of the platform. The company also encourages users to refer friends, offering additional rewards for successful referrals.In summary, Lantern Finance provides a secure, user-friendly platform for staking Ethereum, making it accessible to a wide range of crypto investors. The company focuses on simplicity, security, and regulatory compliance to offer a reliable staking solution.Keywords: Ethereum staking, crypto investors, staking rewards, user-friendly, security, BitGo partnership, regulatory compliance, 15% fee, ETH validation, referral rewards.

column5: Wearable technology ring for stress and mood tracking
column6: Moodring Tech is developing a 'smart mood ring' and accompanying mobile application designed to help users track and understand their emotional state. The core product is a wearable ring that contains biosensors to monitor physiological indicators such as body temperature, heart rate, and electrodermal activity (sweat). This data is then analyzed to provide users with insights into their stress levels and moods in real-time. The company was founded by Lauren Tracy, whose personal experience with a panic attack, brought on by overwork and stress, inspired the creation of the product. Prior to Moodring, Tracy founded a wellness tech startup called Blue Fever, which was a social self-care app for teenagers. This background provided her with insights into the prevalence of stress and the desire for tools to manage emotional well-being. The mission of Moodring Tech is to help users recharge and avoid burnout by becoming more aware of their body's stress signals. The Moodring system consists of the physical ring and a mobile app. The ring discretely logs moods and tracks activities that bring joy, while also automatically detecting stress in the body to offer relaxation guidance. The technology aims to find a balance between sensing bodily stress reactions and the individual's mental feeling of stress. The business model appears to be direct-to-consumer, with plans for an initial collection of 1,000 smart rings available for purchase via reservation on their website. The company is targeting individuals who are looking to manage daily stress, prevent burnout, and design a life with more joy and rest. Keywords: wearable technology, smart ring, mood tracker, stress management, emotional wellness, mental health tech, biosensor, electrodermal activity, heart rate monitoring, wellness technology, Lauren Tracy, burnout prevention, relaxation guidance, joy tracking, self-care, consumer electronics, health-tech, emotional intelligence, biofeedback, personal wellness device

column5: Leadership performance system fusing AI and peer accountability.
column6: ApogeeOS provides a performance system for elite leadership teams operating in volatile environments where traditional strategy cycles are insufficient. The company targets senior leadership teams at corporations, with testimonials from roles such as Chief Talent Officer, CEO & Founder, and CHRO. Its business model appears to be a hybrid of a technology platform and a high-touch coaching or consulting service, delivering a program over a 90-day period designed to create lasting capabilities within the client's team.The core of the ApogeeOS offering is a system that combines human interaction with artificial intelligence to foster alignment, commitment, and performance. It is built on a three-phase methodology: 'Discover' to diagnose performance drivers and blockers; 'Define' to establish and align the team around a core commitment; and 'Drive' to translate that commitment into action through structured sprints. The company states this process installs a permanent capability that teams can manage on their own after the initial 90-day engagement.A central feature of the system is 'The Board Effect,' a mechanism where leaders make commitments openly in rotating peer board sessions. This process of peer accountability, challenge, and support is designed to drive follow-through more effectively than individual coaching models. The technology platform complements these sessions with several tools: 'Generative Reflection' for real-time self-awareness, cross-team feedback functionalities, an 'Intentional Progress Model' to connect daily actions with long-term vision, and a leadership dashboard for management and alignment. The system is grounded in over two decades of research in behavior change, decision science, and leadership development. It is supported by a notable advisory board that includes organizational psychologist Adam Grant, former Google Chief Decision Scientist Cassie Kozyrkov, and psychotherapist Esther Perel.Keywords: leadership performance, AI performance system, peer accountability, executive teams, leadership development, behavior change, decision science, team alignment, corporate strategy, performance management, The Board Effect, generative reflection, leadership dashboard, elite teams, executive coaching, team performance, organizational psychology, operating system for teams, professional development

column5: AI-native platform for automated SOX testing
column6: Midship provides an AI-native platform designed to automate Sarbanes-Oxley (SOX) compliance testing and internal audit workflows for public companies. The company's core offering uses agentic AI to manage the entire SOX testing lifecycle, from following audit plans and performing control tests to evaluating evidence and generating complete, audit-ready work papers. This approach is designed to significantly reduce the manual effort and time auditors spend on repetitive tasks, allowing them to focus on higher-value risk analysis and strategic judgment. The platform is built to work with existing tools and workflows, including a native Excel add-in, to ensure seamless integration without requiring extensive new system training. The business model centers on serving public companies, including major technology firms, social media platforms, and fintechs, that face mounting compliance costs, audit complexity, and talent shortages. By automating the laborious process of evidence collection and control testing, Midship aims to replace the need for costly external consultants and reduce the internal man-hours dedicated to compliance. The platform differentiates itself from legacy systems of record like AuditBoard and Workiva by not just managing workflows, but by automating the actual testing itself with AI agents capable of handling unstructured data and process variability. Midship was founded by CEO Kieran Taylor, CPO Aahel Iyer, and CTO Max Maio. The founders' journey and the company's origin are directly tied to their firsthand experiences with the challenges of SOX compliance at scale. Having supported audit and compliance teams at companies like Instacart, Deloitte, Amazon, Lyft, and PayPal, they came together as a product trio at Dashworks (acquired by HubSpot). The idea for Midship crystallized during the preparations for Instacart's IPO, where they experienced the significant burden of first-year SOX compliance, leading to an internal AI hackathon that revealed the opportunity to automate the entire testing lifecycle. In January 2026, Midship announced a $4.15 million seed funding round led by Costanoa Ventures, with participation from Seguin Ventures, Y Combinator, and other angel investors. This capital is intended to scale the team and accelerate the platform's adoption. Keywords: SOX compliance automation, AI audit platform, internal audit workflows, control testing automation, Sarbanes-Oxley testing, agentic AI, audit workpapers, compliance technology, regtech, continuous monitoring, financial audit AI, evidence collection automation, audit procedures, risk management, Y Combinator, compliance automation, automated workpapers, financial compliance, audit technology, SOX 404

column5: AI-powered spend intelligence for financial services
column6: Sidelight offers an AI-powered intelligence platform designed to help financial services firms manage and optimize their data and application spending. The company provides solutions that deliver transparency and actionable insights into vendor expenses by unifying spend, usage, and value data. Its platform is engineered to identify inefficiencies such as underused licenses, redundant tools, and overpriced data feeds, enabling firms to reduce costs without compromising operational performance. The company's product suite includes a Cost & Subscription Module for real-time spend intelligence, a Contracts AI Module to centralize and search vendor agreements, and a Value Module to benchmark costs and usage against anonymized peer data. By combining user engagement data with AI-driven benchmarks and market insights, Sidelight strengthens its clients' negotiation leverage with vendors, particularly during contract renewals. The platform also helps organizations streamline compliance and risk management by providing a clear audit trail and mapping contract terms to users. Sidelight serves a range of clients within the financial sector, including asset managers, hedge funds, banks, and commodities brokerages. It also offers solutions for database and service providers, delivering independent user feedback to help them gain insights into product satisfaction and identify churn risks. The business model appears to be subscription-based, with different pricing tiers for its various modules. The company was founded by industry experts with decades of experience in finance, data, and AI. column5: B2b invoice platform that helps vendors and enterprises run more efficiently, using email plugins
column6: Hummingbill operated as a B2B invoice and accounts receivable management platform, focusing on enhancing operational efficiency for both vendors and enterprises. The company was founded in May 2014 by Adam Walker. An alumnus of Carnegie Mellon and Rochester Institute of Technology, as well as a Ewing Kauffman Global Scholar, Walker had prior entrepreneurial experience founding a smart meter venture and a small wind turbine company. He leveraged this international business experience to develop Hummingbill's customer base. The company initially launched in Kenya to leverage mobile money and SMS for billing in emerging markets before shifting its focus to India later in 2014 to pursue a larger market. Hummingbill's core product was a software solution, notably featuring a Gmail plugin, designed to streamline the accounts receivable process. This tool automated client follow-ups, generated accounts aging reports, and provided sales teams with direct access to their customers' outstanding invoices within their email interface. For suppliers, the platform offered electronic invoicing and AR management to accelerate payments and create predictable cash flow. For enterprise clients, it aimed to empower accounts payable departments, reduce shared services costs, and improve vendor relationships. The company offered a free tier for single users and a paid enterprise package that included payment gateway integration. Throughout its operation, Hummingbill raised a total of $255,000 in funding from investors including Africa Angels Network, Start-Up Chile, and VentureWell. In a significant milestone, the company was acquired by KredX, an Indian invoice-discounting marketplace, in March 2017 for an undisclosed amount. The acquisition was aimed at strengthening KredX's technological capabilities in accounts receivables. As part of the deal, the Hummingbill team, including its founder, and its 500 merchants were integrated into KredX. column5: Intelligently identifies and classifies day-to-day documents regardless of format
column6: Based in Santo Domingo, Dominican Republic, Tagshelf is a technology firm specializing in artificial intelligence and data processing, founded in 2014 by Raul Roa. The company's inception is rooted in Roa's extensive background as a software engineer with over 15 years of experience and a family history of engineering that fostered a curiosity for how things work. His academic pursuits include a Master of Science in Software Engineering and another in Interactive Technology, which provided the foundation for developing solutions to complex data problems. Tagshelf operates a B2B model, providing a SaaS platform designed to automate and streamline document-heavy processes for enterprises, particularly in the insurance and banking sectors. The company generates revenue by offering its suite of AI-powered tools that eliminate manual data entry, reduce operational costs, and improve efficiency. Key investors include Enlaces, NEXXUS, and Banreservas, who participated in a significant angel investment round, underscoring the growing confidence in the Dominican Republic's startup ecosystem. The company's core offering is an intelligent automation platform that processes documents without human interaction. This is delivered through a suite of distinct products: 'Alfred' focuses on the complete automation of the data processing lifecycle, from ingestion and classification to validation and integration with existing client systems. 'Johnny5' leverages natural language processing to enable communication through non-traditional channels and automate the creation of documents like contracts and reports. '2Face' is a verification platform that uses facial recognition and document analysis to confirm identities, validate documents such as IDs and licenses, and detect fraud. Together, these tools enable clients to classify documents regardless of format, extract relevant information, and integrate that data into their workflows, turning unstructured information into actionable business intelligence. column5: Practice enablement platform for independent ABA therapists.
column6: Tellos provides a practice-enablement platform for Board Certified Behavior Analysts (BCBAs) specializing in Applied Behavior Analysis (ABA) therapy for children with autism. The company was founded by a group of parents and clinicians who experienced firsthand the frustrations and inefficiencies of the traditional ABA therapy model. They aimed to create a solution that supports both families and therapists, addressing issues like long waitlists, therapist burnout, and administrative burdens. Tellos operates on a practice-enablement model, not an employer model. It empowers BCBAs to launch their own independent, remote practices without the typical startup costs and administrative challenges. The company's business model involves managing the operational infrastructure of a private practice, allowing clinicians to focus on care delivery. This support includes client acquisition, hiring and managing Registered Behavior Technicians (RBTs), handling insurance credentialing, billing, scheduling, and payroll. Tellos provides an integrated, all-in-one platform that includes EMR and practice management software, eliminating the need for clinicians to source separate tools. For its clients—families of children with developmental needs—Tellos offers in-home ABA therapy designed to build life skills such as communication and self-regulation. The company emphasizes a no-waitlist approach, aiming to connect families with providers quickly. Therapy plans are personalized, starting with a detailed assessment to create a customized care plan that fits the child's needs and family goals. A key component of the service is parent training, where BCBAs provide weekly guidance to empower parents with ABA techniques to support their child's progress. For BCBAs, the platform offers the ability to run a practice with greater control over their schedule and a lighter caseload, typically a maximum of 6-8 clients. This model is designed to increase therapist earnings by 50-80% compared to traditional clinics and reduce burnout. Tellos is privately owned and has no outside investors, allowing it to focus on quality of care rather than profit-driven metrics. column5: Collaborative email facilities for project management and knowledge sharing
column6: Kubi Software was established in 2001 by a group of former IBM Lotus division employees, aiming to address gaps in the collaboration software market. The company was founded in Lincoln, Massachusetts, by Julio Estrada, an experienced software architect who played a key role in developing Lotus QuickPlace and the Lotus Domino Web Server. Estrada, along with other Lotus veterans like President John Shagoury, identified a need for a more integrated collaboration tool that operated within familiar user environments. The firm's core offering was a product category it termed "Collaborative Email." Its flagship product, the Kubi Client, was designed as a software add-in for widely used email platforms, specifically Microsoft Outlook and IBM Lotus Notes. This tool embedded structured team spaces directly within the user's email interface, allowing teams to manage discussions, share documents, and track tasks without leaving their primary communication application. The system utilized peer-to-peer networking, enabling collaboration without relying on central servers, and incorporated Triple DES (168-bit) encryption to ensure secure communication among team members. Kubi Software's business model was centered on software licensing and services, targeting a diverse client base from small enterprises to larger corporations. The company's revenue was generated through subscription-based access to its project management and CRM-enhancing products. Before its acquisition, the company successfully secured significant venture capital, including a Series C funding round of $8 million from investors such as North Bridge Venture Partners, Lazard Technology Partners, and VIMAC Ventures. In September 2006, Kubi Software was acquired by Kadient, Inc., marking the conclusion of its operations as an independent entity. Keywords: collaborative email, Lotus Notes add-in, Microsoft Outlook add-in, team collaboration software, Julio Estrada, peer-to-peer collaboration, enterprise software, Kadient acquisition, secure team spaces, document management, project management tools, CRM software, email-centric collaboration, business productivity software, enterprise communication, North Bridge Venture Partners, workspace integration, data synchronization, secure messaging, team workflow

column5: AI-based audio processing SDKs for immersive communications
column6: Founded in 2018 by Garrett Gleim and Jim Poore, Immersitech is a developer of patented, AI-based audio processing technologies. Poore, who serves as CEO, draws on his experience as a product owner to guide the team. The Pittsford, New York-based company provides its technology through easy-to-integrate Software Development Kits (SDKs). These SDKs are designed for service providers and developers in sectors such as social entertainment, gaming, business communications, and distance learning. The company's core offerings are the ClearVoice SDK and the SpatialVoice SDK. The ClearVoice SDK utilizes machine learning for real-time noise cancellation, focusing on removing impactful background noises to enhance voice clarity. It also includes features like a voice-centric auto equalizer and automatic gain controls to standardize audio levels among different speakers. The SpatialVoice SDK aims to create a more immersive and natural audio environment by delivering 3D spatial audio enhancements. This technology leverages 3D coordinate source positioning to make it seem like voices are coming from different locations, which can help reduce the mental fatigue associated with virtual meetings. Immersitech's business model is based on providing these multi-platform SDKs to global communication platforms and developers, enabling them to integrate high-quality audio capabilities into their own applications. This approach allows clients to enhance user engagement and satisfaction without needing to build the audio technology themselves. The target market includes unified communications, gaming, social chat, and EdTech platforms. The company's core services include the design and installation of solar photovoltaic (PV) systems, battery energy storage, and electric vehicle (EV) charging stations for commercial, industrial, and residential clients. column5: CUBi - Monitoramento e Gestão de Energia Elétrica para empresas
column6: CUBi Energia provides an energy management platform that leverages Internet of Things (IoT) and data science to help companies optimize electricity consumption and reduce costs. The company primarily serves large electricity consumers, including industrial, commercial, and public utility clients in Brazil. CUBi's business model is centered on its technology platform, which automates energy bill management and provides real-time monitoring of energy use, thereby identifying savings opportunities. It generates revenue through its specialized services and platform access.The inspiration for CUBi originated during the founders' master's program at the Rochester Institute of Technology in New York, USA. Co-founders Rafael Turella, Ricardo Dias, Bruno Scarpin, and Tiago Justino were contracted to conduct an energy audit for a university building. After analyzing four years of collected data, their recommendations resulted in an 11% reduction in energy costs, saving the institution approximately $1 million annually. This success prompted them to apply their knowledge to the Brazilian market, where they identified similar opportunities for reducing excessive energy consumption. The startup was officially founded in São Paulo in 2017.The founders' academic backgrounds are deeply rooted in engineering and sustainable systems. Rafael Turella is an Environmental Engineer from UNESP, Ricardo Dias is an Environmental and Urban Engineer from UFABC, and Bruno Scarpin is a Mechatronic Engineer from USP; all three hold master's degrees from the Rochester Institute of Technology. This collective expertise forms the technical foundation of the company. Before CUBi, Ricardo Dias, the current CEO, gained experience at Braskem in the sustainable development team. The founders' shared passion for efficiency and data science drives the company's mission to advance the energy efficiency market in Brazil.CUBi's core offering is an intelligent and intuitive energy management system. It employs proprietary IoT hardware to connect to industrial equipment, enabling real-time data collection and eliminating manual data entry. The platform automatically captures and processes energy bills from any distributor in Brazil, standardizing the data for analysis. Through its online dashboard, clients can monitor energy consumption, identify cost-saving opportunities related to issues like demand charges, tariff classification, and power factor penalties, and receive automated diagnoses and improvement suggestions. For companies navigating the complexities of Brazil's Free Energy Market (Mercado Livre de Energia), CUBi's platform offers tools and expert guidance to facilitate cost-effective energy procurement. The company has received multiple accolades, including being named the top EnergyTech in Brazil for consecutive years by the 100 Open Startups ranking. In 2021, CUBi secured a R$1.5 million seed investment from firms including Bossanova Investimentos and UniAngels to fuel its expansion.Keywords: energy management, electricity consumption, cost reduction, IoT, data science, energy efficiency, energy monitoring, utility bill management, Brazil energy market, energy analytics, demand management, tariff optimization, industrial energy, commercial energy, sustainable systems, cleantech, energy software, Mercado Livre de Energia, smart metering, ESG, energy data analysis, corporate energy strategy, utility cost control, energy savings

column6: Shuttersong, founded in 2012 by William Agush, developed a mobile application designed to merge digital images with sound. The company was headquartered in Wellesley, MA. Agush, an accomplished marketing and business leader with extensive experience in technology and corporate communications, conceived the idea to enrich the photo-sharing experience. His background includes leadership roles at companies like Prime Computer and Global Knowledge Network, as well as founding Adsonica, a software company focused on interactive audio advertising. The core of Shuttersong's offering was a mobile application that allowed users to combine a photograph with an audio clip, either from their music library or by recording live sound. This created a single, playable JPEG file that could be shared across social networks, email, or text messages. The application launched in September 2013 and saw over 30,000 downloads within its first few months. The design philosophy, driven by Agush's desire for global usability, resulted in a wordless user interface to create an intuitive and seamless process for users worldwide. Shuttersong operated in the competitive mobile photo application market, positioning itself as a richer medium than static photos but simpler than video. The business model targeted both individual consumers for personal sharing and commercial clients, such as brands and digital marketing agencies. For businesses, the platform offered a novel way to conduct social marketing campaigns, enhance fan engagement, and utilize user-generated content. The company secured a total of $2.2 million in seed funding to support its commercial expansion and the development of e-commerce capabilities. This included an initial angel investment of $848K in February 2013, followed by a seed round of $1.38M in January 2014, and an additional $500K investment from executive Gil Anderson, who joined the board of directors. column5: Metaresolver has been acquired by Millennial Media
column6: Metaresolver operated as a technology and data platform focused on the programmatic buying sector for mobile performance marketers. Founded in 2012 by Seamus McAteer and Mike Rowehl, the San Francisco-based company developed a mobile targeting platform that connected media and marketing intelligence with consumer attributes such as device model, location, and content.The founders brought significant industry experience to the venture. Seamus McAteer was a co-founder of M:Metrics, a mobile audience measurement firm later acquired by comScore. Mike Rowehl was the first engineer at AdMob and was also involved with the mobile app discovery platform Chomp, which was acquired by Apple in 2012. This background in mobile analytics and advertising technology shaped Metaresolver's core offering. The platform was designed to analyze large volumes of data to facilitate efficient programmatic mobile media buying for advertisers. Its primary function was to use highly accurate, vetted data to identify and build targetable audiences for marketing campaigns, emphasizing a foundation of clean and precise data.The company's business model centered on providing its platform to mobile ad agencies, ad technology companies, and major mobile publishers. It aimed to deliver efficient inventory access coupled with a high degree of transparency and detailed reporting. In April 2012, Metaresolver secured $1.85 million in a seed funding round from investors including Baseline Ventures, Eniac Ventures, Harrison Metal Capital, and Lerer Hippeau. A significant milestone occurred on April 1, 2013, when Millennial Media, a major mobile advertising platform at the time, acquired Metaresolver in an all-cash transaction. The acquisition was a strategic move by Millennial Media to enhance its data-driven capabilities and programmatic buying solutions. column5: Financial Power - Accrue is the IBM backed platform that coverges big-data & machine-learning for predictive analysis of the financial markets.
column6: Founded in March 2013 by Benoit Brookens III, Accrue is a financial technology firm that operates at the intersection of data science and investment analysis. The founder, also known as Dr. Big Data, brings a wealth of experience in financial technology and mobile strategy, holding degrees from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology and Rochester Institute of Technology, alongside a certification from Columbia Business School. His background includes roles as an advisory board member for TradeStation Group and as a founding member of Forbes Councils. Accrue's core offering is the Almanac™, a proprietary system of intelligence designed to provide investors with an analytical edge. This platform ingests and processes massive volumes of unstructured alternative data—sources of information not traditionally considered in financial analysis, such as news, social media, and satellite imagery. The Almanac™ functions as a vast database of world events, enabling users to analyze the cause-and-effect relationships between geopolitical occurrences and reactions in the financial markets. The platform allows clients, likely institutional investors and financial analysts, to query complex scenarios, such as identifying which specific stocks react within a 48-hour window following a particular geopolitical event. By structuring and analyzing data from disparate sources, Accrue aims to uncover non-obvious correlations and deliver actionable intelligence that moves beyond the limitations of traditional financial statements and market data. The company's work in this domain earned it recognition as one of the Top 7 Global FinTechs by the UK Government and the London Stock Exchange. Keywords: alternative data, investment intelligence, financial analytics, geopolitical risk, market reaction, quantitative analysis, investor tools, fintech, system of intelligence, unstructured data, event-driven analysis, Benoit Brookens III, Almanac, stock market analysis, predictive analytics, financial data science, investment research, institutional finance, big data in finance, market intelligence
