column6: Cinema Sala is a non-profit organization focused on providing a platform for Filipino filmmakers to present their work to a worldwide audience. The organization was co-founded by journalist and publicist Vivien Lulim (also cited as Vivien Lou Villalon), producer and director Jon Jon Augustavo, and advertising creative and producer L.A. Lim. This Los Angeles-based entity operates with the mission to foster a greater appreciation for Philippine cinema and culture. The organization's primary activities involve curating and presenting Filipino films through various channels. Cinema Sala hosts global screenings, film festivals, and utilizes an online platform to make these films accessible to a broader viewership. This dual approach of physical and digital distribution allows it to reach diverse audiences and connect the Filipino diaspora with its cultural heritage. By showcasing works from both emerging and established filmmakers, the organization plays a role in the discovery and promotion of Filipino talent on the international stage. Its events, such as the inaugural film festival in Los Angeles in 2018, serve as key venues for cultural exchange and industry networking. As a non-profit, Cinema Sala is run by a passionate and dedicated team of volunteers and likely relies on donations, sponsorships, and grants to fund its operations, including event production, platform maintenance, and filmmaker support. column5: AI-powered personal knowledge and memory management platform
column6: MyMemo AI is a knowledge and memory management platform founded in 2024 by Lingfei Li. The company provides a service that helps users store, structure, and intelligently retrieve their digital content. Li, who previously led product design for intelligent systems at ByteDance and robotics firm AgibotGo, created MyMemo AI to address the challenge of retaining vast amounts of digital information. His background in industrial design, human-computer interaction, and multimodal AI informs the company's vision of creating a "second brain" for both humans and their AI agents. The platform allows users to consolidate various types of digital content, including PDFs, websites, meeting recordings, voice notes, and screenshots, into a centralized, searchable knowledge base. Using large language models like GPT-4o, MyMemo AI transforms this content into interactive "memory blocks" that users can query through a natural language chat interface. A distinct feature is "MemoCast," which converts uploaded content into short audio summaries for on-the-go learning. The system supports multiple languages and uses advanced image recognition for visual content. MyMemo AI operates on a freemium-to-subscription business model. While a free version is available with certain limitations, paid tiers offer increased content uploads, more extensive AI chat capabilities, and support for multiple AI models, including Gemini and Claude. The company targets a diverse client base, including students, researchers, content creators, and professionals. Since its launch in early 2025, it has gained over 100,000 users. The company is also developing an AI Agent SDK and Memory API to allow third-party developers to integrate its memory infrastructure into other applications, positioning itself as a foundational "memory-as-a-service" provider. Keywords: knowledge management, AI assistant, personal knowledge base, digital memory, information retrieval, content summarization, natural language query, multimodal content, AI-powered search, second brain, memory agent, Lingfei Li, MemoCast, knowledge recall, AI collaboration tool, data organization, productivity tool, information management, visual knowledge management, AI agent SDK

column5: Automated football pool management and tracking platform.
column6: Pick the Game LLC provides a platform designed to simplify the organization and management of football pools for social circles such as friends, family, and coworkers. The service was born out of the founder's personal experience of witnessing his father's time-consuming efforts to manage the family football pool using spreadsheets. The platform is designed to eliminate the logistical hassles of running a pool, such as chasing down picks and manual score tracking, thereby allowing users to focus on the competitive and social aspects of the game. The company operates on a straightforward business model, charging a fee per player for its services. For Pick'em, Confidence, and Survivor pools, the fee is $1 per player, charged at the start of the third week of the season. Squares boards are priced at $10 per board. The platform is accessible via its website, pickthegame.com, and through dedicated mobile applications for both iOS and Android devices. The service offers several types of football pools, including Pick'em, Confidence, and Survivor pools, as well as game Squares for various football events. Key features of the platform include automated tracking of picks and standings, real-time scoring updates during games, and email notifications for pick deadlines and results. The system supports custom scoring rules to tailor the game to a specific group's preferences and can handle an unlimited number of participants in a pool. To facilitate management, multiple administrators can be assigned to a single pool, and invitations can be sent easily via a link, QR code, or email. column5: Boonle connects students with businesses on graphic and web design-based projects
column6: Boonle, founded in 2014 by Antonio Calabrese, operated as a marketplace for creative freelancers. Initially based in Rochester, United Kingdom, the company aimed to connect individuals and businesses needing small-scale digital services with freelancers looking to build their portfolios. Calabrese's journey into creating Boonle stemmed from his own early struggles as a freelancer, where he found it difficult to secure clients without a substantial portfolio and often worked for reduced rates or for free to gain experience. The platform's initial model allowed project posters, termed 'Authors', to receive work from freelancers, or 'Producers', on a first-come, first-serve basis. Instead of traditional bidding, this system was designed to help new freelancers, including graphic designers, developers, and writers, gain experience and build a reputation. Compensation was structured around appreciations, reviews, and monetary tips left by the authors after project completion. Boonle's business model was to take a 20% commission from each tip. Over time, the company shifted its focus. An update in early 2017 moved the platform to concentrate exclusively on design projects, with a particular emphasis on connecting US-based college students with businesses. This pivot also saw a significant change in the operational model, moving away from the 'first-come, first-serve' system to an application and invitation-based process. This allowed clients to review student profiles and portfolios before hiring, and for students to apply for projects they were interested in. Consequently, payments were required upfront before work began to secure both parties. Despite these efforts and pivots, the company is now listed as deadpooled.

column5: Development of hypoallergenic latex from annual rubber plant in North America
column6: Nova-BioRubber Green Technologies Inc. operates at the intersection of agriculture and material science, focusing on the production of hypoallergenic and sustainable bioproducts. The company was founded by Dr. Anvar Buranov, who serves as President and CEO. Dr. Buranov possesses extensive research and development experience in industrial crops, with a particular focus on rubber-bearing plants for over 15 years. His academic background includes an MSc in Organic Chemistry from Rochester Institute of Technology and a PhD in the same field, complemented by postdoctoral work at the Pacific Agri-Food Research Center in Canada. The company's core business revolves around the cultivation and processing of Taraxacum kok-saghyz (TKS), a species of Russian dandelion. This plant is a renewable source for biolatex, biorubber, and inulin. Nova Biorubber has developed and patented a green processing technology to extract these materials. This mechanical process operates at room temperature without the use of chemicals, offering significant cost savings in energy, water, and labor compared to traditional methods. The primary market driver is the demand for hypoallergenic latex, as a notable percentage of the general population and an even higher percentage of healthcare workers experience allergic reactions to latex from the Hevea rubber tree. Third-party testing has confirmed the hypoallergenic properties of Nova Biorubber's biolatex. Nova Biorubber's business model targets manufacturers of rubber and latex products, providing them with a sustainable and allergy-free raw material. The applications are extensive, ranging from medical products like gloves and catheters to consumer goods such as mattresses, yoga mats, and balloons. Beyond rubber, the process also yields inulin, a polysaccharide fiber used in the food industry. The company engages with farmers for the cultivation of TKS, which matures in just four months, and seeks industrial partners and investors to scale its operations and establish a North American biorubber industry. The company has received funding from various Canadian government entities, including the Investment Agriculture Foundation BC and the National Research Council Canada. Keywords: hypoallergenic latex, biorubber, TKS, Taraxacum kok-saghyz, sustainable materials, inulin, green processing, rubber alternatives, biolatex, medical grade rubber, agricultural technology, renewable bioproducts, latex allergy solution, dandelion rubber, specialty chemicals, sustainable agriculture, industrial crops, biomaterials, food ingredients, cleantech

column5: AI assistant for content creators
column6: Fanalytics operates as an AI-powered sales and engagement platform tailored for content creators, a sector projected to exceed $500 billion by 2027. The company was co-founded by Steve Klein and Shaun K., a tech entrepreneur with a background in enterprise software. Shaun K. identified the market need after observing friends in his creator network become overwhelmed with managing fan interactions as their popularity grew. The core of Fanalytics' offering is an AI assistant designed to automate and scale fan interactions while maintaining the creator's unique voice and authenticity. Its behavioral AI learns a creator's specific communication style, tone, and decision-making patterns to manage thousands of fan interactions simultaneously, 24/7. This allows the platform to handle direct messages, respond to fans in real-time, and distribute personalized content, ensuring no interaction or revenue opportunity is missed. The service is particularly beneficial for individual creators on paywall platforms and for agencies that manage multiple creator accounts, enabling them to scale operations efficiently. The business model is primarily performance-based. Fanalytics charges a small monthly fee plus a percentage of the additional revenue generated by its service. One co-founder stated this is typically a 10% cut of the revenue the platform generates for the creator, which often amounts to less than 3% of the creator's total earnings. A key feature is the dynamic pricing capability, where the AI analyzes audience behavior to recommend and adjust content prices to maximize revenue. In May 2025, it was announced that Bell Buckle Holdings, Inc. (OTC: BLLB) acquired an equity stake and gross revenue participation in Fanalytics through a merger with MORE. At that time, the platform had already been tested by over 250 creators in its beta phase, facilitating over 5 million fan interactions. Keywords: creator economy, fan engagement, AI chatbot, automated content delivery, influencer tools, dynamic pricing, creator monetization, audience management, social media automation, fan interaction, AI sales agent, personalized content, creator analytics, subscription platforms, digital content sales, Bell Buckle Holdings, Steve Klein, Shaun K., automated messaging, revenue optimization

column6: Drone.io is a container-native Continuous Integration (CI) platform designed to automate the software build and testing process for development teams. It integrates seamlessly with multiple source code management systems such as GitHub, GitHub Enterprise, Bitbucket, and GitLab, making it versatile for various development environments. Drone.io supports multiple operating systems and architectures, including Linux x64, ARM, ARM64, and Windows x64, and works with any language, database, or service that runs inside a Docker container. This flexibility allows developers to choose from thousands of public Docker images or provide their own, tailoring the CI pipeline to their specific needs.Drone.io primarily serves busy development teams looking for a self-service CI solution to replace aging systems like Jenkins. The platform's ease of use and simplicity have led to its adoption in over 5,000 self-hosted installations. Following its acquisition by Harness, Drone.io continues to operate as an open-source platform, with significant investments planned to enhance its community, platform, and mission.The business model of Drone.io revolves around providing a robust CI platform that helps development teams build and test software quickly and securely. By automating these processes, Drone.io reduces the time and effort required for software development, thereby increasing productivity and efficiency. The company generates revenue through its open-source model, supplemented by investments from its parent company, Harness.Keywords: Continuous Integration, Docker, Source Code Management, Automation, Development Teams, Open Source, Jenkins Replacement, Software Testing, Multi-Platform, Self-Service CI.

column5: Creating, selling, and trading non-fungible tokens (nfts)
column6: ITSMYNE, a brand under NFT Labs, Inc., operates as a social-plus marketplace focused on officially licensed sports non-fungible tokens (NFTs). Founded in July 2021 by Atharva Sabnis and Ayan Uali, the company aims to simplify NFTs for a mainstream audience, particularly those not native to cryptocurrency. The platform is designed to be user-friendly, allowing individuals with or without prior crypto experience to purchase digital collectibles. The core of ITSMYNE's business is to provide a centralized marketplace where sports and cinema fans can buy, trade, and discuss officially licensed NFTs directly from athletes, teams, and production studios. This model seeks to bridge the gap between traditional social media and blockchain-based economies. Revenue is generated through several streams, including a per-user fee from blockchain partners for onboarding new users, a percentage of client campaign spending on their web3 advertising platform, and fees for their Itsmyne product. The platform supports payments in both fiat currency and cryptocurrency and provides an integrated custodial wallet to ease the user experience. A key feature of the platform is its social component, which is structured similarly to Reddit, allowing community members to engage in discussions about their favorite topics while trading relevant NFTs. ITSMYNE emphasizes offering curated collections of officially licensed NFTs, ensuring authenticity for buyers. In November 2021, its parent company NFT Labs announced it had raised nearly $2 million through a combination of seed, strategic, private, and public funding rounds, with investors including Shima Capital, AU21 Capital, and Oracles Investment Group. The project also introduced its native BEP-20 token, MYNE, on the BNB Smart Chain, which is intended for staking, governance, and other ecosystem functions. Keywords: NFT marketplace, sports NFTs, digital collectibles, fan engagement, web3 platform, NFT Labs, officially licensed NFTs, social commerce, crypto collectibles, blockchain technology, MYNE token, digital memorabilia, fiat and crypto payments, custodial wallet, fan community, sports technology, entertainment NFTs, Atharva Sabnis, Ayan Uali, BEP-20

column5: AI-native test automation for e-commerce agentic flows
column6: Geck AI is a platform provider specializing in agentic commerce solutions for the e-commerce sector. The company assists online stores in optimizing for a new channel of traffic driven by AI agents with high purchase intent. Geck’s full-stack platform addresses three main areas: Brand Monitoring, Answer Engine Optimisation (AEO), and Agentic Experience Optimisation (AXO). The Brand Monitoring service tracks mentions, citations, and recommendations of a brand across various AI models like ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, Claude, and Bing. The Answer Engine Optimisation (AEO) functionality focuses on implementing structured data and other signals that help AI models parse website information, turning a brand's pages into authoritative sources. Lastly, the Agentic Experience Optimisation (AXO) service identifies and resolves issues such as broken selectors, bot blocks, and cart flow problems that hinder AI agents from completing transactions. Geck offers a technical friction audit that provides a score and a prioritized list of blockers to fix. For developers, Geck provides an AI-native test automation platform available as a VS Code extension and a Command Line Interface (CLI). This tool writes, runs, and maintains end-to-end tests. Its key features include self-healing tests that adapt to UI changes and the ability to generate test scripts from plain English descriptions, eliminating the need for brittle selectors. The platform validates e-commerce flows across autonomous browsers and AI systems like ChatGPT Atlas and Perplexity Comet. Keywords: agentic commerce, e-commerce optimization, AI agent traffic, answer engine optimisation, AEO, agentic experience optimisation, AXO, brand monitoring, AI test automation, self-healing tests, VS Code extension, CLI, structured data, bot blocking, e-commerce analytics, AI-native testing

column5: An enterprise-class cannabis technology platform that manages risk and compliance easily
column6: Komplyd operates as a compliance-as-a-service (CaaS) platform specifically developed for the cannabis industry. The company was founded in 2020 by Rose Elliott and Audrey Fernandez-Elliott. The founders leveraged their extensive backgrounds in building data-driven solutions for highly regulated industries. Rose Elliott brought over two decades of experience in engineering and product from roles at PayPal, Netflix, and Tenable, while Audrey Fernandez-Elliott had a background in managing complex M&A integrations and global expansion at companies like PayPal, Apple, and Fitbit. The firm's core offering is an automated platform that provides a single, developer-friendly API integration to manage the complex and varied compliance requirements across multiple jurisdictions, including state, county, and local municipalities. Komplyd's business model is centered on its API-first platform, which enables clients, ranging from small operators to large multi-state operators (MSOs), to embed regulatory compliance features and analytics directly into their applications. This is accomplished through an AI-driven compliance engine and a common data model that establishes protective guardrails and automated workflows. The platform addresses the intricate data problem posed by cannabis regulations, allowing product development teams to ensure their applications are compliant, thus reducing a significant barrier to entry and expansion in the market. A significant milestone for Komplyd was the closing of an oversubscribed Seed Round in August 2022, co-led by Poseidon and Achari Ventures, which was intended to fund its national expansion. Subsequently, on June 6, 2023, Komplyd was acquired by Green Check Verified (GCV), a fintech provider of compliant cannabis banking solutions. This acquisition was strategic for GCV, aiming to integrate Komplyd's capabilities with seed-to-sale technologies to better connect the cannabis plant's supply chain with the commerce supply chain, a critical step for future interstate and international trade. The integration enhances data connectivity and provides more profound insights for cannabis operators on the Green Check platform. Keywords: cannabis compliance, compliance-as-a-service, CaaS, regtech, cannabis technology, API integration, multi-state compliance, regulatory compliance, seed-to-sale, cannabis banking, fintech, automated compliance, Rose Elliott, Audrey Fernandez-Elliott, Green Check Verified, Poseidon Ventures, Achari Ventures, cannabis regulations, data model, compliance engine

column5: NuNee is a device that can significantly reduce knee discomfort associated with patellofemoral pain
column6: NuNee is a company dedicated to providing innovative solutions for individuals suffering from patellofemoral pain syndrome (PFPS), commonly known as runner's knee. Founded by Mike Emmerling, a triathlete and engineer who experienced knee pain for over five years, NuNee offers a unique product designed to relieve pressure between the patella (knee cap) and femur (thigh bone). This pressure relief is crucial in alleviating the primary cause of PFPS. The company's primary market includes athletes, particularly runners, and individuals experiencing knee pain due to overuse. NuNee operates in the health and wellness sector, focusing on non-invasive pain relief solutions. The business model revolves around direct-to-consumer sales through its website, nuneeshop.com, and strategic partnerships, such as the one with Runners Connect, to enhance brand visibility and reach. Revenue is generated through the sale of the NuNee device and related training kits. column5: Social mobile platform for daily competitive puzzles.
column6: Puzzlit is a mobile-based social puzzle platform founded in 2023. The company was co-founded by a team of graduates from Lehigh University, Rochester Institute of Technology, and Penn State, including Matthew Hayden, Evan Chen, Christopher Banas, Patrick Curley, and Jack Catlett. The founding team combines backgrounds in industrial engineering, finance, software development, and product design, born from a shared passion for puzzles. The company operates in the entertainment software and mobile gaming market, targeting casual gamers and puzzle enthusiasts. Puzzlit's business model is centered on a free-to-play mobile application, currently available on Apple devices, with plans to expand to Android. Revenue is generated through a premium subscription model and in-app purchases. The 'Puzzlit Premium' subscription offers access to an archive of past puzzles, ad-free gameplay, and advanced analytics. The app also includes in-app purchases for items like avatars and gold. The core product is a mobile app that provides users with over 13 different types of daily puzzles, ranging from word and number games to logic and strategy challenges like 'ChainIt', 'WordIt', and 'SortIt'. What distinguishes Puzzlit is its integration of social features, which transforms a typically solitary activity into a community-driven experience. Users can create an account to connect with friends, share results, compete on leaderboards, and track performance statistics. The platform also features a proprietary 'Puzzlit IQ' algorithm to assess a user's puzzle-solving efficiency. column5: AI-powered workflows for manufacturing knowledge retention.
column6: LeanFlow provides a knowledge management system for manufacturers, designed to capture and standardize critical process information. The company was founded by Sundeep Boinpally, who serves as the Product Lead, and Kirsten Gallagher, the Co-Founder and Adoption Lead. Both founders have extensive backgrounds in manufacturing and process improvement; Boinpally is an industrial engineer with over 15 years of experience, while Gallagher, known as 'The Kaizen Ninja', has led over 150 kaizen events in medical manufacturing, defense, and industrial operations. Their shared experience on manufacturing floors led them to create a solution for the persistent problem of 'tribal knowledge'—uncodified expertise that is lost when experienced operators leave a company.The company's core product is a platform that transforms unstructured documents like PDFs and spreadsheets into structured, mobile-ready Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) using artificial intelligence. This allows manufacturing teams to create visual, step-by-step workflows directly from the factory floor, accessible to operators via QR codes on any device. The system includes features such as photo and video integration, annotations, version control, approval workflows, and a full revision history to ensure procedures are current and audit-ready. By digitizing and standardizing this operational knowledge, LeanFlow aims to reduce the onboarding time for new hires, minimize process variation between shifts, and streamline audit preparations. LeanFlow's business model appears to be centered around a service or subscription model, targeting manufacturing companies of various sizes. They offer to convert a client's high-impact processes into these operator-ready workflows, providing a tangible starting point for new customers. The company is based in Austin, Texas, with a presence in Greenville, South Carolina. Keywords: manufacturing knowledge management, workflow automation, standard operating procedures, SOP software, tribal knowledge capture, process standardization, industrial engineering, lean manufacturing, operator training, continuous improvement, kaizen, AI in manufacturing, digital work instructions, mobile-first workflows, audit readiness, quality control, knowledge retention, operational efficiency, manufacturing technology, Sundeep Boinpally, Kirsten Gallagher, process documentation, visual workflows, QR code procedures, manufacturing AI

column5: Secure, controllable on-premise and air-gapped AI infrastructure
column6: Turtosa provides enterprise AI infrastructure that allows organizations to deploy, monitor, and secure their use of artificial intelligence models. The platform is designed for companies in highly regulated sectors such as finance, healthcare, legal, and defense, addressing the risks of unmanaged AI adoption. It offers the flexibility to deploy on-premise, in a private cloud, or in a completely air-gapped environment, ensuring complete data control and sovereignty. The core of Turtosa's offering is a portal that provides a unified interface for employees to access multiple leading AI models, including those from OpenAI, Claude, and Gemini, as well as self-hosted local models. This centralized system enables organizations to manage access, monitor usage in real-time, track costs, and enforce compliance with regulations like HIPAA and GDPR. Key features include role-based access controls, real-time analytics, cost tracking, keyword alerts for sensitive data, and immutable audit trails for compliance reporting. The platform also integrates with enterprise authentication systems like Active Directory and Okta for seamless single sign-on. A significant feature is its Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) capability, which allows the AI models to securely connect with and search internal knowledge bases such as OneDrive, Slack, and email. This enables employees to get intelligent, context-aware answers based on the company's private data without that data ever leaving the secure environment. By providing robust monitoring and control, Turtosa aims to prevent data leaks, ensure regulatory compliance, and give businesses the visibility needed to manage their AI usage effectively. Keywords: enterprise AI infrastructure, on-premise AI, air-gapped AI, secure AI access, AI governance, large language model management, compliance monitoring, data sovereignty, multi-model AI hub, retrieval-augmented generation, AI security, LLM portal, AI usage monitoring, data control, private cloud AI, unified AI interface, HIPAA compliant AI, GDPR compliant AI, enterprise authentication, role-based access control

column5: Swayfund is the develop crowd sourced influencer marketing platform using blockchain oracle for verification
column6: Swayfunded.com, operating as Sway Funded, positions itself as a proprietary trading firm that provides traders with access to simulated capital accounts. The firm was established in February 2024, aiming to create an experience-focused platform for traders. Its business model centers on offering challenges that traders must pass to gain access to a funded account. This model allows individuals to trade in a simulated environment that mirrors real market conditions without risking their own personal capital. Revenue is generated through one-time fees paid by traders to participate in these evaluation challenges.  Clients of Sway Funded are aspiring and experienced traders who seek to leverage larger amounts of capital than they might personally have available. The firm offers funded accounts with balances up to $200,000. To qualify, traders must complete an evaluation process, which typically involves achieving specific profit targets within certain drawdown limits, though there are no time limits to pass the challenges. Once a trader successfully passes the evaluation, they become an "SF Trader" and are eligible to earn rewards of up to 90% of the simulated profits they generate. The company promotes fast reward payouts, claiming to process them within 24 hours.  Since its inception, Sway Funded has pursued an aggressive growth strategy through acquisitions. In July 2024, it acquired MyFlashFunding, adding over 5,000 traders to its platform. This was followed by the acquisition of Glow Node's user base in August 2024 and Karma Prop in September 2024. By July 2025, the company reported that its community had grown to over 70,000 traders. The platform allows trading across a wide range of instruments, including Forex, commodities, stocks, indices, and cryptocurrencies. The service is accessible to a global audience, with support offered in more than 10 languages.  Keywords: proprietary trading firm, prop firm, funded trader program, trading challenges, simulated trading, trader evaluation, profit sharing, forex trading, commodity trading, stock trading, crypto trading, remote trading, trading capital, zero-risk trading, financial markets, trader funding, performance-based funding, MyFlashFunding acquisition, Glow Node acquisition, Karma Prop acquisition, SF Trader, trading rewards

column5: Automated URL redirect platform for one-to-one matching
column6: Redirects.net provides a URL redirection platform designed to automatically generate precise one-to-one matches for any 301 redirect. This service is aimed at brands, agencies, consultants, and developers seeking to save time and reduce costs associated with managing URL changes. The core function is to seamlessly forward users from one web page to another, a crucial process for maintaining search engine optimization (SEO) value when a website's structure is altered, a page is moved, or a domain is changed. The platform specializes in server-side 301 redirects, which signal a permanent move to search engines, helping to preserve link equity and avoid broken links. This is particularly useful in scenarios such as migrating a website to a new domain, correcting typos in marketing campaign URLs, or consolidating multiple domains under a single website. By automating the creation of optimal redirects, the tool aims to enhance user experience and prevent traffic loss. The business model appears to be subscription-based, offering different tiers of service. For example, similar services in the market provide free plans with a limited number of redirects and basic analytics, scaling up to paid plans that include advanced analytics, bulk import/export features, and API access for developers. The service is built to be a hassle-free alternative to self-hosting and managing redirects, offering features like automatic HTTPS, analytics on redirect usage, and path forwarding to maintain the original URL structure in the destination. Keywords: URL redirection, 301 redirects, SEO management, domain migration, link equity, automatic redirect, path forwarding, URL forwarding, redirect management, SEO tools, developer tools, digital marketing, website maintenance, URL management, dead links, traffic management, website migration, brand management, cost-saving tools, time-saving tools

column5: Online platform that enables internet devices to be able to communicate with its api
column6: iOTOS, founded in 2012 by Tony Pietrocola, Art Geigel, and Christopher Armenio, operated as a wireless connectivity solutions enabler based in Shaker Heights, United States. The company's genesis stemmed from the founders' hobbyist-level interest in pairing smartphones with everyday objects to control them remotely. This tinkering evolved into a business concept centered around the burgeoning "Internet of Things" market. The venture was developed through the LaunchHouse Accelerator Program, which also participated in its seed funding round.  iOTOS developed a wireless device named NiOs, designed to enable consumer electronics to connect to WiFi networks. This technology was intended to allow manufacturers of products, machines, medical devices, or appliances to offer their customers control and monitoring capabilities via a mobile device. The core offering converted a mobile device into a remote control for various connected items. The company initially targeted hardware hackers with an easy way to control hobby projects but soon discovered a broader commercial demand. Its business model involved selling this technology to other companies, such as garage door opener retrofit packagers, and it also initiated pre-sales for its own product, the NioGarage, a retrofit WiFi garage door system.  The company secured $25,000 in a single seed funding round on November 28, 2012, with LaunchHouse as the sole institutional investor. At its peak, iOTOS had a team of four full-time employees, including the founders, and three part-time staff. The company also received recognition, being named the “Most Promising Startup” at the 2013 NEOSA Tech Week's Best in Tech awards. According to market analysis, iOTOS is now considered a deadpooled company.  Keywords: wireless connectivity, Internet of Things, IoT, remote control, smart home, WiFi module, NiOs, consumer electronics, hardware, appliance control, mobile device monitoring, LaunchHouse, retrofit systems, garage door opener, wireless hub, machine control, medical device connectivity, NioGarage, Shaker Heights startup, NEOSA Tech Week

column5: AI-driven insights for strategic business execution.
column6: ElevateForward.ai, founded in 2024 and based in Austin, Texas, provides a strategy and execution platform designed for leadership teams in growing businesses. The company was established to address the common operational friction where fragmented data, disconnected tools, and stalled initiatives hinder progress. It aims to bridge the gap between gaining insights and implementing effective action, a challenge founder Leah Read witnessed firsthand while advising executives and growth-stage companies. Her background, with degrees from UCLA and UC Berkeley School of Law, gave her a perspective on the time and opportunities lost to manual processes and scattered systems, inspiring the launch of ElevateForward. The company's business model is software-first, offering two primary engagement tiers. The first is a standalone 'Insight Report,' an AI-powered diagnostic analysis tailored to a specific business challenge, such as defining KPIs or modeling growth, which is delivered within five business days. This service allows businesses to gain targeted clarity without a subscription. The second tier is a subscription to the 'Elevate Studio' platform, a centralized application designed for continuous strategy management. This platform enables teams to organize insights, translate them into strategic priorities, structure initiatives with clear ownership, and track progress through a 90-day execution roadmap. ElevateForward.ai is built for founders, COOs, and leadership teams across various sectors, including SaaS, healthcare, financial services, and manufacturing. The platform is positioned as an alternative to traditional consulting, offering speed and accessibility without the high cost and long timelines. For service providers like consulting firms and agencies, the company also offers white-label options, allowing them to use the framework to generate strategic reports for their own clients. column5: Connected electric bikes for urban mobility
column6: Cowboy is a Belgian company that designs and manufactures connected electric bikes tailored for urban riders. Founded in Brussels in 2017 by Adrien Roose, Karim Slaoui, and Tanguy Goretti, the company's mission is to foster mindful movement and transform urban mobility. The founders combined their expertise in business and engineering to address a gap in the market for e-bikes that were not only dependable but also stylish and technologically integrated. The business operates on a direct-to-consumer model, primarily through its website, and has expanded its reach through an omnichannel strategy that includes partnerships with over 250 independent bike retailers across Europe and the US for sales and after-sales service. Cowboy's e-bikes are known for their sleek, minimalist design and smart features. The core product is an electric bike connected to a mobile app, which provides users with features like a secure key, live stats, GPS tracking, and fitness data. The company has continually refined its products, launching the Cowboy 3 in 2021 with more advanced functionalities. To enhance the ownership experience, Cowboy offers services such as "Cowboy Care," a subscription-based maintenance and repair service, and "On-Demand" services for at-home support. It also introduced "Cowboy Circular" for the sale of certified refurbished second-hand bikes. The company has a strong focus on in-house research and development, aiming to control critical technologies and manufacturing processes to maintain a competitive edge. Since its inception, Cowboy has secured significant funding, including a $80 million Series C round in 2022, bringing its total funding to $120 million to fuel growth and product innovation. column6: Based in Gray, Maine, WOOLIGANS specializes in do-it-yourself (DIY) slipper kits made from sustainable and recycled materials. The business targets a wide range of customers, from individual crafters to groups looking for activities, such as school fundraisers and adult education programs. The company's business model is built on making crafting approachable and satisfying, with every kit made on demand. column6: My Special Days, operating as SpecialDays.com, functions as a digital platform centered on event reminders and gift inspiration. The service was conceived after the founder repeatedly forgot significant dates, including his wife's birthday, and struggled with finding suitable gifts. This personal pain point led to the creation of a centralized solution to help users remember and celebrate special occasions. The platform is free for users and serves as a reminder service for important events like birthdays and anniversaries. The core business model revolves around affiliate partnerships and providing a curated selection of gift ideas, experiences, days out, and getaways. Revenue is presumably generated through commissions or referral fees when users make purchases through the partnered brands featured on the platform. The service delivers inspirational content and offers directly to users' inboxes and through a mobile application, aiming to simplify the process of celebrating important life moments. The platform provides gift guides for various recipients and occasions, along with discounts and offers from its partners to make the celebrations more affordable. The primary offering is a free reminder service that sends notifications to users' inboxes or mobile devices, ensuring they do not miss important dates. Users can also share significant dates with family and friends through the platform. Beyond reminders, the site provides extensive content categorized into 'things to do' and 'gift inspiration'. This includes guides for activities and gift ideas tailored to different relationships and preferences, from tech-loving dads to sisters. By partnering with a variety of brands, SpecialDays.com provides users with access to discounts and special offers, adding a value proposition of cost savings to the emotional benefit of remembering and celebrating special moments. column5: AI-powered digital identity protection and content monitoring
column6: NILSOLUTIONS.AI offers an AI-powered software-as-a-service (SaaS) platform designed to protect an individual's or brand's digital identity. The service continuously scans social media, websites, and online marketplaces to detect unauthorized use of a person's name, image, and likeness (NIL). It utilizes advanced machine learning to identify deepfakes, impersonations, and other forms of AI-generated content. When unauthorized use is detected, the platform provides users with instant alerts and reports, along with built-in legal tools to facilitate content removal. These tools include cease-and-desist letter generators and other resources to enforce intellectual property rights. The company's services are aligned with recent legislative efforts like the 'Immobilizing Technological Deepfakes on Websites and Networks Act' (TAKE IT DOWN Act), which mandates online platforms to remove non-consensual deepfakes and similar content upon notification. NILSOLUTIONS.AI serves a diverse client base that includes content creators, influencers, celebrities, public figures, and corporate executives seeking to protect their personal brand and public image from digital impersonation, fraud, and reputational harm. column6: Ahaan Solar is the renewable energy division of Airox Nigen, an Indian company with a history in industrial gas and hydrogen systems since 1994. Ahaan Solar was incorporated as a private limited company on January 1, 2025, with directors Anilkumar Ramprasad Agarwal and Aniruddh Anilkumar Agrawal. It also offers OEM manufacturing for export-ready modules. The company's primary product is solar panels built on TOPCon architecture, which offers higher efficiency, better performance in various conditions, and lower degradation compared to older technologies. column5: Mobile application neutralizing deepfakes and impersonation attacks
column6: Real Recognizes Real AI provides a mobile application designed to combat identity-based digital attacks like deepfakes and targeted impersonations. The company's technology is built to establish and maintain trust among individuals and organizations by enabling them to authenticate their digital contacts. The application utilizes a combination of cryptographic algorithms, biometric authentication, and memory-based security questions to verify a person's identity before digital communication occurs. This approach, which the company calls memory-based authentication, turns shared memories between trusted contacts into a security key, a method they've termed The Doppelgänger Protocol™️. The startup was founded by Jonathan Bateman, a student at the Rochester Institute of Technology (RIT) studying applied math, computing security, anthropology, and sociology. His academic background focuses on understanding how people use and manipulate technology, with an emphasis on security, privacy, and ethics. The venture is supported by RIT's Gap Year Entrepreneurship Fellowship Program, which provided Bateman with a $20,000 stipend and business coaching. This fellowship, established by RIT alumnus Austin McChord, aims to foster entrepreneurial thinking and provide real-world advantages to students. The company aims to address the rising threat of AI-driven scams, such as CEO fraud and family emergency scams using voice clones, which have resulted in significant financial losses. Instead of relying on AI detection algorithms, which can be part of an endless technological arms race, Real Recognizes Real AI focuses on proactive verification. The primary product is a free mobile app that functions as a trusted contacts application. Before a call, message, or video chat can begin, the app confirms the identities of both parties instantly and privately. This serves as a form of multi-factor authentication for people, using shared experiences as a core verification method. The company's business model appears to be focused on providing this application directly to consumers, with plans to launch on the iOS app store. By personalizing security methods typically used in commercial enterprises, Real Recognizes Real AI aims to offer a robust solution for individuals to protect themselves from increasingly sophisticated impersonation attacks. Keywords: deepfake neutralization, impersonation attack prevention, biometric authentication, cryptographic algorithms, memory-based security, digital identity verification, trusted contacts app, multi-factor authentication for people, The Doppelgänger Protocol, AI security, voice clone protection, digital trust, identity verification, anti-fraud technology, mobile security, cybersecurity, personal security, Jonathan Bateman, Rochester Institute of Technology, Gap Year Entrepreneurship Fellowship

column6: Real Power provides customized critical power solutions across a range of sectors including utilities, healthcare, government, financial, education, manufacturing, and transportation. The company is also involved in manufacturing power-take-off (PTO) driven, chassis-integrated mobile electric generator systems. The company's founding members include Peter Wachtell, Toby Prehn, and Rodney Jones, who all serve as managing members. Peter Wachtell has a background in the asset-backed and commercial mortgage-backed securitization industry since 1985 and has co-founded businesses in medical software and renewable energy production. Toby Prehn holds a BS in Business Administration from the University of California, Berkeley, and owns companies in manufacturing, construction, hospitality, and finance. column6: Roviero is a Silicon Valley-based deep-tech startup targeting the edge artificial intelligence (AI) compute market. Founded in 2019 by industry veterans and serial entrepreneurs Deepak Mital, Sumat Mehra, and Ravi Setty, the company develops semiconductor IP and software to enable AI processing on edge devices. The founding team's collective experience in the semiconductor and AI industries underpins the company's approach to creating power-efficient processing solutions. In February 2021, Roviero secured $6.6 million in an oversubscribed seed funding round led by Great Point Ventures, with participation from Monta Vista Capital and Dolby Family Ventures, to expand its development efforts.  Roviero's business model centers on licensing its intellectual property (IP) and software stack to semiconductor companies and original equipment manufacturers (OEMs). The company's main offering is a full-stack AI accelerator solution that includes both hardware design and the necessary software for implementation. This approach allows clients to integrate Roviero's technology into their own System on Chip (SoC) designs, facilitating natively intelligent devices like cameras, IoT products, mobile phones, and computers. By providing FPGA-based platforms, Roviero enables customers to start developing applications before their final SoC is ready, potentially shortening time-to-market.  The company's flagship product is the CortiOne™ Neural Processing Unit (NPU), complemented by the CortiSoft™ compiler technology. This hardware and software combination is engineered for high performance with ultra-low power consumption and low latency, which are critical for edge devices. The CortiCore™ processor architecture is designed for power efficiency and can scale from under one to over 500 TOPS (trillion operations per second). A key feature is the inclusion of an Arm Cortex-M core deep within the NPU, offering the flexibility to support future neural network requirements. This hybrid design simplifies compiler complexity and improves hardware utilization, aiming to address scalability and programmability challenges in edge AI.  Keywords: edge AI, AI accelerator, neural processing unit, NPU, semiconductor IP, system on chip, SoC, low-power AI, AI inference, graph processor, CortiOne, CortiSoft, deep-tech, computer vision, IoT devices, intelligent edge, hardware-software co-design, AI compute, embedded systems, machine learning

column5: Orphidia is a handheld point-of-care diagnostic device that tests for a number of chronic diseases. It’s portable, as accurate as lab machines anywhere and not just in a heavily controlled clinical environment. It works like a glucometer, a fast pric
column6: Orphidia was a medical diagnostics company that developed a portable system for performing rapid blood tests. The technology was designed to deliver lab-quality results from a single drop of blood in just 20 minutes, directly at the point of care, such as a doctor's office or pharmacy. The system aimed to decentralize blood testing, making it faster, more accessible, and more affordable compared to traditional centralized laboratories. The company's device was intended to automatically perform a wide range of common blood tests. The company was founded in 2013 by Aron Rachamim and aimed to disrupt the blood-testing market dominated by giants like Quest Diagnostics and LabCorp. Orphidia was based in San Francisco, California. Despite its ambitions, the company appears to have ceased operations. The founder, Aron Rachamim, has moved on to found other ventures, and the company's online presence, including its website, is no longer active, with the domain being for sale. column5: Mobile phone payments. Backed by Telefonica.
column6: PayMins, founded in Dublin, Ireland, in 2012 by Patrick Walsh, operated as a mobile payment platform. The company positioned itself as a solution for smaller organizations, content creators, and developers to monetize their products through direct carrier billing. The core service enabled end-users to purchase digital goods or content, with the cost conveniently added to their monthly mobile phone bill. This approach targeted a market segment that required a straightforward method for processing mobile transactions. The business model was designed to simplify the sales process for vendors who could integrate the system to start accepting payments without complex setups. PayMins successfully secured $185,000 in total funding over two seed rounds. The initial round of $65,000 occurred in December 2012 from Wayra, Telefónica's startup accelerator. This was followed by a subsequent seed round of $120,000 in July 2013, with participation from Techstars. Despite this early backing and a clear value proposition in the then-emerging mobile payments space, the company has since ceased operations and is considered deadpooled. column6: Dflyco.com operates as a web-based utility offering image compression and conversion services. The primary function of the site is to reduce the file size of images, with a specific feature to compress images to 20kb, and to convert PNG files into the JPG format. The platform is designed for users who need to optimize images for web use, improve storage efficiency, or ensure compatibility across different platforms. The service is accessible directly through its website, dflyco.com. Users can upload a PNG file, which the tool then automatically converts into a high-quality JPG format using its compression algorithms. The website emphasizes a balance between file size reduction and the preservation of image quality. It also provides informational content to help users understand the differences between PNG and JPG formats to make informed decisions based on their needs, such as whether transparency (PNG) or smaller file size (JPG) is the priority. The business appears to generate revenue through advertising, as indicated by its privacy policy which mentions the use of third-party vendors like Google and DART cookies to serve ads to site visitors. A YouTube video from 2013 does mention Dflyco as a deaf-owned sports apparel company, suggesting a possible pivot or that the domain is now owned by a different entity. column6: DMR Design & Build Sdn. Bhd. is an interior design and construction company headquartered in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, that has been operating since 2010. column5: Global non-profit inspiring girls in STEM subjects
column6: greenlight for girls (g4g) is an international non-profit organization dedicated to encouraging girls of all ages and backgrounds to pursue studies and careers in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM). The organization was founded by Melissa Rancourt, an engineer and entrepreneur, who started g4g with a single email to gauge interest in the mission. This query led to the formal establishment of the organization within 12 months, backed by over 500 volunteers across every continent. Rancourt, who holds a BS in Industrial Engineering, an MBA in Entrepreneurship, and has a background in creative writing, channels her experience as an engineer and business leader into the organization's vision. The non-profit is headquartered in Brussels, Belgium, with offices in the US. The organization's business model is based on providing its programs for free, supported by donations and corporate partnerships to cover costs like transportation, materials, and equipment, ensuring accessibility for all girls, including those from less advantaged communities. The core of g4g's activities are its 'g4g Day' events, which are one-day, hands-on workshops led by volunteer role-models in STEM fields, demonstrating the fun and creative side of science. These events can host from 100 to 500 attendees. Beyond its flagship events, g4g runs several other programs, including 'g4g in the classroom,' tailored learning projects, a metaverse lab, on-air broadcasts with role models, and hackathon-style design challenges. Operating on six continents, the organization has reached over 70,000 students through more than 480 events in numerous countries, creating a global community of over 13,500 role models. Keywords: STEM education, girls in science, non-profit, youth empowerment, gender equality in STEM, STEM workshops, educational outreach, hands-on learning, volunteer-led, global education, community events, engineering for girls, technology for girls, science for girls, mathematics for girls, STEM role models, youth development, educational charity, STEM advocacy, social enterprise

column5: Enzymatic technology for recycling laminated paper waste
column6: Flexoresearch Group is a Thailand-based research and development company focused on the pulp, paper, printing, and packaging industries. Founded in 2008 by Paijit Sangchai, the company started as an incubatee in Thailand Science Park's technology business incubation program. Sangchai, motivated by the environmental problems of papermaking, developed a novel enzymatic technology to recycle laminated paper waste, which was previously often burned in Thailand. The company's core technology, developed in 2010, uses unique blends of enzymes to separate materials like plastic and aluminum from paper in waste products such as milk cartons and sticker back liners. This process recovers pulp that can be used as a safer and cheaper replacement for asbestos in construction materials. For every 300 tons of coated paper processed, the technology yields 270 tons of recycled pulp, plastic, and metals. Beyond its laminated paper recovery enzyme, the firm also provides other solutions including deinking enzymes, fiber strengthening enzymes, and consulting for corrugated mills. Flexoresearch Group operates on a technology licensing business model. The company's innovation gained international recognition when it was named a Technology Pioneer by the World Economic Forum in 2011. By October 2012, the company had licensees in 32 countries and was expanding with facilities in locations like South Africa, Malaysia, and the UK. Keywords: laminated paper recycling, enzymatic technology, pulp and paper industry, waste valorization, cleantech, environmental services, recycling technology, corrugated packaging solutions, deinking enzyme, pulp mold, anilox roll maintenance, paper waste, technology licensing, sustainable materials, circular economy, waste to wealth, biochemical R&D, green technology, packaging waste, industrial enzymes

column5: Klora Health Supplements | Gut-Mind- Body formulas to help achieve human homeostasis
column6: Klora operates as a direct-to-consumer wellness brand with a specific focus on the connection between gut health, mental function, and overall physical well-being. The company was co-founded in 2022 by Dr. Kirten Parekh, a surgeon, alongside Dr. Jonathan Snow and Daniel Snow. Dr. Parekh's personal health journey, which began with severe microbiome disruption from antibiotics in 2016, was the primary catalyst for the company's creation. This experience led him to pursue a degree in nutrition and delve into the science of the microbiome, ultimately shaping Klora's mission. The company's name is derived from the concept of “Clean Flora.” The business model is primarily subscription-based, offering savings and convenience to its customers for monthly or quarterly deliveries of its products. Klora's revenue is generated through online sales of its dietary supplements directly from its website, getklora.com. The company targets health-conscious consumers who are experiencing issues like bloating, indigestion, fatigue, and brain fog. In March 2022, Klora secured $1 million in a seed funding round to support its growth. Klora's product line centers on scientifically formulated supplements manufactured in cGMP compliant, FDA-registered facilities in the United States. Its flagship product, Gut-Renew, features a distinctive capsule-in-capsule technology that combines a prebiotic and a postbiotic. This formulation is designed to nourish beneficial gut bacteria and support the cells of the digestive tract. Another key product, Bloat-Digest, is a digestive enzyme supplement intended to help break down proteins, starches, and other food molecules to alleviate bloating and improve nutrient absorption. The products are formulated with clean, non-GMO, and traceable ingredients, avoiding synthetic fillers. To ensure scientific credibility, Klora has established a scientific advisory board that includes a Ph.D. in nutrition and a neurophysiologist. column5: Integrated IP-centric technology solutions for enterprise clients
column6: Vision Technologies Inc., founded in May 2000 by John Shetrone Jr. and Kevin Nolan, is a national systems integrator for commercial and federal clients. The company began in a single room above a garage and achieved $800,000 in revenue in its first year with 10 employees. By 2020, Vision reached over $1 billion in aggregate revenue since its inception, employed over 550 people, and projected annual revenue of $150 million. Vision offers design, installation, support, and project management for IP-converged technologies. Its core services include audio-visual solutions, data centers, security systems, wireless infrastructure (including Distributed Antenna Systems), structured cabling, and professional/managed IT services. The firm serves a diverse client base in sectors such as government, healthcare, education, and finance. Its business model is centered around its Integrated Technology Delivery™ (ITD™) methodology, which provides comprehensive, customized technology solutions. The company operates as a Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Business (SDVOSB), which facilitates procurement for government entities through vehicles like the GSA Schedule. Significant milestones include winning its first national rollout with Grainger in 2004, securing its GSA Schedule 70 contract in 2005, and landing a major 8-year, $100 million contract with the EPA in 2009. The company has been consistently recognized on the Systems Contractor News (SCN) Top 50 Systems Integrators list. Headquartered in Glen Burnie, Maryland, Vision has expanded to multiple locations across the United States. column5: AI-powered software for local energy grid simulation
column6: Local Energy Technologies provides an AI-powered software platform for designing, simulating, and managing local energy grids. The company's services cater to industries and communities, helping them to optimize energy consumption, reduce emissions, and facilitate peer-to-peer energy trading. The design and simulation tools allow users to model optimal grid infrastructure and assess the financial viability of energy projects. column5: Creating a better way to connect people with the products and services they need
column6: BeyondTrucks is a forward-thinking company that provides smart transportation management software designed specifically for the trucking industry. The company aims to address the inefficiencies and high costs associated with outdated trucking software, which can cost American fleets an average of $9,700 per driver annually. BeyondTrucks offers a cloud-based platform that simplifies the management of trucking operations, finances, and personnel through an intuitive and user-friendly interface.The primary clients of BeyondTrucks are carriers and fleets of various sizes that are either undergoing a transition or are mandated by investors or customers to modernize their operations. These clients often face fragmented workflows and rely on manual processes and spreadsheets, which BeyondTrucks helps to eliminate through customized smart workflows. The platform integrates analytics and automation to streamline communication and collaboration between dispatchers, drivers, and customer service teams, resulting in improved efficiency and happier personnel.BeyondTrucks operates in the transportation and logistics market, focusing on providing a modern alternative to legacy Trucking Management Systems (TMS). The company's business model revolves around offering a subscription-based service where clients pay for access to the software and its various features. This model ensures a steady revenue stream while providing continuous updates and support to the clients.The company differentiates itself from traditional TMS by being SOC2 compliant, meaning it adheres to strict data security standards. Additionally, BeyondTrucks can sync with most Single Sign-On (SSO) providers, making it easier for businesses to integrate the software into their existing systems without significant changes to their operations.In summary, BeyondTrucks is revolutionizing the trucking industry by providing a comprehensive, easy-to-use software solution that reduces costs and improves operational efficiency for carriers and fleets.Keywords: trucking software, fleet management, cloud-based, automation, analytics, cost reduction, user-friendly, SOC2 compliant, transportation, logistics.

column5: Product lifecycle management solutions for process manufacturers
column6: Formation Systems provides product lifecycle management (PLM) solutions tailored for process manufacturing companies in sectors like food and beverage, pharmaceuticals, and home and personal care. The company was acquired by Infor, and later key resources and founders evolved into Oak Barrel Software, which continues to work on the Optiva PLM application originally developed by Formation Systems. The firm's core offering, now known as Infor PLM for Process (Optiva), helps businesses to develop new products, optimize formulas by analyzing material costs and manufacturing capacity, and ensure regulatory compliance for labeling. The PLM software integrates with Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems, which allows for streamlined operations and data consistency across applications. The platform provides services that include a discovery process, implementation, documentation, training, and ongoing customer support. It is designed to manage the entire product lifecycle, from initial concept through design, manufacturing, service, and disposal. By unifying product data and processes, the system aims to help companies accelerate development, reduce costs, and improve product quality. Historically, Formation Systems was recognized as a leading PLM vendor, particularly for its solutions for food and beverage clients, before being acquired. The team from the original Formation Systems has continued to provide services for the Optiva application, including global deployments, integrations, and extensions through Oak Barrel Software. Keywords: product lifecycle management, process manufacturing, formula optimization, regulatory compliance, PLM software, ERP integration, food and beverage, pharmaceuticals, Optiva PLM, new product development

column5: Skigit | Live, Share... Make the Difference!
column6: Skigit Inc. operates a video-centric social networking platform designed to foster connections between consumers and brands through user-generated content. Founded in 2013 by Frank Spina, who also serves as the company's President and CEO, Skigit is headquartered in Land O' Lakes, Florida. The company's core offering is a platform where users create and share short video skits, known as "Skigits," to showcase products and services they appreciate. The business model centers on turning these authentic user-created videos into promotional assets for brands. This approach allows companies to leverage earned media and boost engagement by featuring genuine customer testimonials. The platform serves as a conduit for brands to identify and recognize their most enthusiastic advocates, effectively turning everyday consumers into influencers. For users, the platform offers a space to connect with both the brands they admire and a broader audience. Users can participate by recording or uploading 30-second videos, or by linking YouTube videos, through the Skigit mobile application. A key feature of the platform is the "SkitBit" incentive system, where brands can offer rewards to users for creating content about their products. These incentives can be monetary or donated to a charity of the user's choice, adding a philanthropic dimension to the user-brand interaction. The platform also encourages collaboration by allowing users to "plug" videos together, creating mashups called "Superskigits." Skigit began with a seed round in January 2013 and later engaged in equity crowdfunding in February 2014 to support its growth. Keywords: user-generated video, brand engagement, social networking platform, consumer-to-brand marketing, earned media, video testimonials, influencer marketing, adtech, social commerce, mobile video, customer advocacy, brand promotion, Frank Spina, product reviews, short-form video, consumer engagement, SkitBits, Superskigits, digital advertising, social media marketing

column5: Etalia Pizza - Fresh-Baked Artisan Crust, Organic, Gluten-Free.
column6: Etalia Foods, founded in 2008 and headquartered in Boulder, Colorado, operates within the food products and e-commerce sectors, specializing in artisanal, gluten-free pizza and bread. The company's inception is rooted in the personal health journey of its founder, Kristin Carman. Following a diagnosis of gluten intolerance, which was triggered by the physical stress of thru-hiking the Appalachian Trail, Carman, a New Yorker with high pizza standards, was disappointed with the existing gluten-free options on the market. This experience drove her to develop a product that did not compromise on taste or texture. The company's primary offering is a bake-at-home pizza characterized by a fresh dough that rises and bubbles when cooked, creating a crust that is crispy on the outside and chewy on the inside. This differentiates it from many other frozen pizzas which are typically pre-baked. The product line includes topped pizzas like Margherita and Verdura (vegetable and ricotta), as well as a plain crust called 'The Nudo' for customers who prefer to add their own toppings. Etalia also offers a Rustic Boule artisan bread. A key aspect of their product strategy is the use of high-quality, certified organic, and non-GMO ingredients, such as a signature sorghum-based flour blend. The dough is also plant-based and free from the top 8 allergens, catering to a wide range of dietary needs. Etalia Foods targets health-conscious consumers and individuals with dietary restrictions who are seeking high-quality, convenient meal options. The business generates revenue through direct-to-consumer online sales via its website and distribution through retail partners, including Whole Foods stores in Colorado and select Natural Grocers locations. The company's financial history includes an Angel investment in 2017 and a small amount of funding from an accelerator/incubator program in 2023. column6: Agitare Technologies, Inc. is a product development and consulting company that specializes in emerging cloud technologies like containers (Docker and Kubernetes), Internet of Things (IoT), cognitive services (OCR, Vision and bots) and machine learning. We are Microsoft Gold Partner for Cloud Platform.  Verticals we specialize in include Financial Services, Healthcare, Manufacturing, Retail and Telecommunications. We have solid background in the security, regulatory and compliance requirements in those industries including standards like SOX, PCI-DSS, HIPAA, HiTrust, GDPR and CPNI, and can provide you with the necessary guidance when deploying your solutions to the cloud.  As former Microsoft employees, our leadership team has very close relationships with the Azure product groups, which allows us to gain early access to new Azure features and capabilities and guide you better into your cloud journey. In addition, we have significant experience designing and deploying solutions on other cloud providers like AWS and Google Cloud and are able to advise you on how to deploy multi-cloud solutions.  Our engagement model includes:  - Assessment of your current cloud deployments and strategy including architecture, security, application delivery and cost structure  - Cloud preparedness consisting of workshops, training and proof of concept deployments  - Design and architecture of your cloud infrastructure and applications leveraging cloud-native services  Our infrastructure and application designs are built from the ground up with security, automation, monitoring and cost-savings in mind.

column5: Mesh technology | Connecting things and people | Wyld Networks
column6: Wyld Networks AB is a virtual satellite operator addressing the critical gap in global IoT connectivity. Recognizing that only about 15% of the Earth's surface has terrestrial network coverage, the company provides solutions to connect IoT devices across the remaining 85%. The company was established in Cambridge, UK, in 2016 and is a wholly-owned subsidiary of the publicly traded Wyld Networks AB, listed on the Nasdaq First North Growth Market. The leadership team includes CEO Alastair Williamson, who brought extensive experience from the telecommunications industry when he joined in 2019, and CTO and Co-Founder Gene Myers. The core of Wyld's business is enabling sensor-to-satellite communication through its suite of products. The primary product, Wyld Connect, consists of hardware modules and terminals that use the LoRaWAN protocol to send data from IoT sensors directly to a constellation of Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellites. This hybrid solution allows devices to switch seamlessly between terrestrial networks and satellites, ensuring 100% global coverage. Complementing the hardware is Wyld Fusion, a cloud-based software platform for managing and visualizing IoT data from both satellite and terrestrial networks. Wyld Fusion is also offered as a standalone SaaS product. The company's business model is twofold, mirroring that of a mobile network operator. It generates revenue through the one-time sale of its Wyld Connect modules and terminals, and creates a recurring revenue stream by charging a monthly data subscription fee of up to $5 per connected device. Wyld targets clients in sectors with remote operations, such as agriculture, energy, utilities, supply chain, and environmental monitoring. It has established partnerships with major entities like Eutelsat, Chevron, and Bayer for trials and deployment of its technology. The company is expanding its technological capabilities, having introduced support for S-band and L-band frequencies to offer higher bandwidth and bidirectional communication, thereby broadening its addressable market. column5: Providing a secure, end-to-end cashless payment solution for institutions and parents
column6: Founded in 2012, Airpay Payment Services Pvt. Ltd. operates as an omnichannel financial services platform headquartered in Mumbai, India. The company was established by Kunal Jhunjhunwala, Amit Kapoor, and Rohan Deshpande, all of whom had previously worked together at Hungama Digital Media Entertainment. The initial idea stemmed from Jhunjhunwala's experience at Hungama, where he encountered challenges with high commissions and clunky integrations for micropayments, revealing a systemic issue for many businesses in India. This led to the creation of Airpay with a mission to simplify payments and make financial services more inclusive across both urban and rural India. Kunal Jhunjhunwala, who serves as the Managing Director, is a serial entrepreneur with a background that includes roles at Hungama and E-Phoria Technologies, as well as being the nephew of the late investor Rakesh Jhunjhunwala. The company faced a significant crisis before the pandemic, with slumping business and the departure of its two co-founders, but Jhunjhunwala led a turnaround. By the 2021-22 fiscal year, the company had achieved a net profit of Rs 21 crore, a significant recovery from a loss of Rs 85 crore the previous year. For the fiscal year 2023-2024, Airpay reported total revenue of ₹129.1 Cr and a profit after tax of ₹9.8 Cr. Airpay provides a software-as-a-service (SaaS) platform that enables businesses to accept and process payments across a multitude of channels, including online, in-store, mobile, and via payment links. The platform supports over 160 payment instruments, such as credit and debit cards, net banking, UPI, mobile wallets, and cash. The company's business model targets both consumer-to-business (C2B) inbound payments and business-to-business (B2B) outbound payments. Revenue is generated through its payment processing services, and it has diversified to include value-added services like lending, loyalty programs, and business management tools to create multiple revenue streams. Airpay serves a diverse client base across sectors like hospitality, education, banking, and retail. The platform offers an integrated dashboard that provides real-time transaction visibility and advanced analytics. Key features include multi-currency support, recurring billing, fraud prevention, and point-of-sale (POS) transaction capabilities. A distinctive aspect of Airpay's strategy is its focus on empowering traditional businesses and extending financial services to underserved rural and semi-urban areas through its 'airpay vyaapaari' network of micro-entrepreneurs. This network enables last-mile access to services like cash withdrawals, money transfers, and bill payments. In 2023, the company acquired the personal financial management startup FinMapp, relaunching it as airpay money to further its financial inclusion goals. column6: Jumpwire Media operates as a specialized production and marketing firm, focusing on delivering services for virtual, hybrid, and in-person events. The company primarily serves mission-driven organizations, such as non-profits and associations, helping them to plan and execute high-quality conferences, summits, and webinars. The core of Jumpwire Media's business is its full-service event production, which is managed by a team of former TV producers who apply broadcast-quality standards to their projects. This service covers all technical and logistical aspects, including stage and AV production, multi-camera video, sound, lighting, live streaming, and on-site technical management to ensure seamless execution. For virtual and hybrid events, the company offers features designed to boost audience participation, such as polls, breakout rooms, and live Q&A sessions. In addition to production, Jumpwire Media provides comprehensive event marketing services to drive attendance and engagement. This includes creating tailored marketing strategies, managing organic and paid social media campaigns across platforms like Facebook and LinkedIn, and executing email and CRM campaigns. Their approach is to build a complete marketing funnel that captures audience attention before, during, and after the event, converting attendees into brand advocates. The business model centers on providing these integrated production and marketing services as a complete package, allowing clients to focus on their core mission without getting bogged down by technical complexities. column5: Churn Labs focuses on building internet and mobile products with the assistance of entrepreneurs
column6: Churn Labs, founded in 2015 by Sean LaFlam, operated as a web-based solution aimed at helping mobile device users reduce their bills and find better service plans. The company, which is now defunct, was based in Hawthorne, United States.The core service allowed users to input their needs or upload their actual mobile bill to the company's website, heychurn.com. The platform would then analyze the user's current plan, requirements, and usage patterns to deliver personalized recommendations. This process was designed to guide users toward better quality services and achieve cost savings. Beyond individual users, Churn Labs also intended to provide insights to mobile operators based on the data it captured, with the goal of helping them reduce customer churn.By October 2016, the company's website was no longer operational, and the company is listed as deadpooled. Churn Labs did not have any recorded funding rounds.Keywords: mobile bill reduction, mobile plan optimization, customer churn analysis, telecom cost savings, personalized mobile plans, mobile service recommendations, usage trend analysis, telecom data insights, heychurn.com, Sean LaFlam
